title: Sabrina Elba & Ashley Thomas To Star In Rom-Com ‘Not Like Us’ From Ramla Ali And Richard A. Moore
full_text: Skip to main content By Zac Ntim Zac Ntim International Reporter @ZacharyNtim More Stories By Zac Simon Field Dies: Former Rotterdam Film Festival Head & ICA Cinema Director Was 80 Giovanni Veronesi’s ‘Dio Ride’ To Close Venice Film Festival ‘S.W.A.T.’ Creator Aaron Rahsaan Thomas & ‘Origin’ Producer Paul Garnes Among Names Set For Inaugural Ghana Creative Economy Initiative View All July 21, 2026 4:00am Sabrina Elba & Ashley Thomas Momodu Mansaray/Getty Images. Writer-director duo Ramla Ali and Richard A. Moore have wrapped production in East London on their debut feature Not Like Us , a romantic comedy starring Sabrina Elba and Ashley Thomas . The film follows a Somali single mother who defies her family and culture by falling in love with a Jamaican man. “The official synopsis reads: Amina Hassan (Elba) is a forty-something pharmacist and a single mother who has built everything herself: her business, her reputation, her beautiful, controlled life, and she would like it to stay that way. Edmund Clarke (Thomas) has other ideas. Related Stories Casting Finn Wittrock & Ashley Thomas Join FX's Witness Protection Pilot News 'In The Shadows:' First Look At Jasmine Jobson Suited Up As Ramla Ali In Boxing Biopic, Altitude Taking Pic To Cannes “Complicating matters are the three community aunties, Hawa, Safia, and Maryam, who have decided that what Amina needs is a husband. Never mind that their own marriages could generously be described as works in progress. Not Like Us is a story about love, desire, and the right to choose your own happiness, told entirely from within the community it depicts.” Sofia Oxenham also stars in the film. Watch on Deadline Ali and Moore co-wrote and co-directed Not Like Us . The pair have a direct connection with the material. The pair married in 2016, and Moore, a white British man, converted to Islam. Ali was born in Mogadishu, Somalia, and grew up in East London. Ali is also a former Olympic boxer. Her life will be chronicled in the feature biopic In The Shadows, which will be released in UK cinemas in October. Not Like Us was independently financed after what Ali and Moore described as difficulties working within the traditional British funding infrastructure. Sunita Mirchandani Hyams and Luke Hyams of Pangaea Group produced the feature alongside Andrew Conrad and Gina Carter of 22 Summers, with Ali and Moore. The executive producer is Richard Fearn of Affine Films. The production team is aiming for a 2027 launch. title: Action Xtreme Expands Into U.K., Ireland Distribution With John Hannah-Starring ‘Bad Day at the Office’ (Exclusive)
full_text: × Skip to main content By Lily Ford Plus Icon Lily Ford @lilyfordtweets Follow Sign Up View All July 21, 2026 4:00am John Hannah in 'Bad Day at the Office' Action Xtreme Action Xtreme is expanding into the U.K. and Ireland film distribution space with the release of the John Hannah-led Bad Day at the Office . The adrenaline-fueled POV action thriller, produced and directed by Chee Keong Cheung and also starring Radha Mitchell ( Silent Hill , Man on Fire ), will receive its U.K. premiere at the Edinburgh International Film Festival in August. It’s set to open the prestigious Midnight Madness strand before launching nationwide in cinemas across the U.K. and Ireland this October. The move represents a significant milestone for the U.K.-based studio. Alongside an expanding production slate, Action Xtreme is working on international partnerships, talent development initiatives and its pioneering MA in Action Filmmaking with Falmouth University. In addition to overseeing the theatrical release of productions in the U.K. and Ireland, the company will also selectively acquire commercially driven action and genre titles that “align with the brand.” Related Stories Movies 'My Friend the Porn Star' Review: German Documentary About Desire Is a Kooky, Kinky Delight Movies Toronto-Bound 'Onwards and Sideways,' With Laura Linney and Rhys Ifans as People Being Diagnosed With Parkinson's, Gets New Images (Exclusive) Bad Day at the Office is shot entirely from the first-person perspective using GoPro cameras. The film follows a man (Hannah) who wakes in a hotel with no memory of who he is, only to discover a dead body in the bath and an army of assassins closing in. Forced to go on the run and uncover the truth while fighting for survival, he discovers he may not be the man he thought he was. “Shot in the style of first-person shooter video games with cine-literate wit, humor, ferocious pacing and a cracking soundtrack, this is an anarchic, ultra-violent and hilarious thrill ride of pure, cinematic carnage,” said festival director at Edinburgh Emma Boa. “The ultimate Midnight Madness experience.” Cheung, also CEO of Action Xtreme, added: “ Bad Day at the Office is exactly the kind of bold, cinematic experience Action Xtreme was created for… Seeing our first theatrical release launch with a festival premiere at Edinburgh’s Midnight Madness, before rolling out nationwide, makes this an incredibly exciting moment for the company.” Co-CEO Andreas Roald said distribution has been “a key component” of their strategy. “Over the last few years we’ve focused on building Action Xtreme into far more than a production company… We’ve created a platform that develops action filmmakers, produces films and now brings those films directly to action audiences. Distribution has been a key component of our strategy.” The studio’s recent productions include Nigerian action thriller Son of the Soil , which became one of Netflix Africa’s most successful action releases following theatrical runs across multiple territories. Upcoming productions include sci-fi thriller The Experiment , starring Rhona Mitra and Famke Janssen, as well as several international co-productions currently in development. THR

title: Ryan Reynolds Says There Will ‘Eventually’ Be Another ‘Deadpool’ Movie: ‘There’s a Few Really Deep Cuts That I Think Are Missing’
full_text: Jul 21, 2026 3:56am PT Ryan Reynolds Says There Will ‘Eventually’ Be Another ‘Deadpool’ Movie: ‘There’s a Few Really Deep Cuts That I Think Are Missing’ By Ellise Shafer Plus Icon Ellise Shafer Latest Michael Urie on Returning to ‘Oh, Mary!,’ His Second ‘Shrinking’ Emmy Nod and 20 Years of ‘Ugly Betty’: ‘That Show Was So Ahead of Its Time’ 20 hours ago Andy Burnham Set to Become U.K. Prime Minister as Former Manchester Mayor Named Labour Party Leader 4 days ago Donald Trump Drops Part of $10 Billion BBC Lawsuit 4 days ago See All ©20thCentFox/Courtesy Everett C Deadpool ‘s story isn’t over yet. During a panel at Fanatics Fest over the weekend, Ryan Reynolds confirmed that there will “eventually” be another movie starring the Marvel antihero — but it may revolve around “deep cuts” from the character’s comics. “There’s a few really deep cuts that I think are missing from the movies. I think there’s a few deep cuts missing from some of the comics,” Reynolds told moderator Nature Luvz You (via Hobby Nonsense ). “There’s certainly some stuff that I love that [Deadpool co-creator] Fabian [Nicieza] wrote that is incredible [and] Gerry Duggan, another friend and incredible comic writer, that we can put in. But then there’s stuff upcoming. There’s eventually, you know, remotely another ‘Deadpool’ film. So it’s going to be great.” Related Stories Fox's Red Seat Ventures, Amazon Strike Programmatic Advertising Partnership Ubisoft Hires Former Amazon Games Chief Christoph Hartmann as Head of New Division, Including Oversight of Acquired 'March of Giants' Game Marvel did not immediately respond to Variety ‘s request for comment. Popular on Variety Following the huge success of 2024’s “Deadpool & Wolverine,” Reynolds told “Sunday Today” in April that he’s already started writing Deadpool’s return to the big screen, though he said the Merc With the Mouth wouldn’t be the main focus. “I have some stuff kind of written, but I don’t think I am ever going to center him again,” Reynolds said. “He is a supporting character. He is a guy who is great in a group.” Reynolds has also downplayed rumors that the character makes a comeback in the upcoming “Avengers: Doomsday” film, though speculation remains rife about the possibility. In a 2024 interview with The Hollywood Reporter , Reynolds said he’d love for the character to cross paths with the Avengers or X-Men — but doesn’t want him to join either group. “If he becomes either, we’re at the end,” Reynolds said. “Deadpool works so well appearing with the X-Men and Avengers, but he always needs to stay an outsider. His ultimate dream is to be accepted and appreciated. But he can’t be accepted. His coping mechanism of deflecting shame through humor works only when used to pave over his many inadequacies. If and when he does become an Avenger or X-Man, we’re at the end of his journey.” From ‘Heated Rivalry’ to ‘Ponies,’ Many of the Year’s Best Roles Required Challenging Accent Work Love Is on the Air: From ‘Nobody Wants This’ to ‘Bridgerton,’ TV’s Buzziest Shows Mix Romance With Prestige Walk on the Wild Side: Comedy Series Like ‘Matlock’ and ‘The ’Burbs’ Star Funny Women in Dark Situations Loading comments... title: Lisa Nandy Stays in Post as U.K. Culture Secretary, Kanishka Narayan Appointed First AI Minister
full_text: Jul 21, 2026 3:03am PT Lisa Nandy Stays in Post as U.K. Culture Secretary, Kanishka Narayan Appointed First AI Minister By K.J. Yossman Plus Icon K.J. Yossman @KJYossman Latest Lisa Nandy Stays in Post as U.K. Culture Secretary, Kanishka Narayan Appointed First AI Minister 1 hour ago World Cup Final Ratings Skyrocket Across Europe, Despite England and France Losses 19 hours ago ‘The Odyssey’ Opening Weekend Beats ‘Star Wars: The Last Jedi’ to Break BFI Imax Records in U.K. 20 hours ago See All Getty Despite new Prime Minister Andy Burnham undergoing an extensive cabinet reshuffle, incumbent culture minister Lisa Nandy is set to remain in post. Nandy, who has been a member of Parliament since 2010 as representative for the constituency of Wigan, was appointed secretary of state for culture, media and sport by previous Prime Minister Keir Starmer in July 2024, shortly after the Labour party won the general election, making her the 12th minister in the post in almost as many years. Related Stories Ubisoft Hires Former Amazon Games Chief Christoph Hartmann as Head of New Division, Including Oversight of Acquired 'March of Giants' Game Fox's Red Seat Ventures, Amazon Strike Programmatic Advertising Partnership Her two-year stint has also made her one of the longest-serving. She previously served as the shadow cabinet minister for international development and shadow housing minister. Popular on Variety Nandy took office during a period of uncertainty for the U.K.’s screen sector in the wake of the Hollywood writer and actor strikes, post-pandemic inflation coupled with a downturn in the ad market and slashed budgets from broadcasters and streamers. She is currently overseeing the BBC’s royal charter renewal, including its funding mechanism, as well as the U.K. arm of the Paramount/Warner Bros. Discovery merger, in which she said she was “minded to intervene.” Other major deals in the U.K. include Comcast-owned Sky’s recent acquisition of public service broadcaster ITV, which has yet to receive regulatory approval, as well as potential bidders for its soon-to-be-spun-off production arm ITV Studios. Meanwhile Burnham has also appointed Kanishka Narayan as the first minister for AI, jointly in the Cabinet Office and the Department for Business, Innovation, Science and Trade. Narayan was previously parliamentary under-secretary of state in the science, innovation and technology department. He was elected in July 2024 as the MP for Vale of Glamorgan. title: BBC Staff In Town Hall Revolt Over 1% Pay Rise, Job Cuts & Romesh Ranganathan Ad: “It’s Insulting”
full_text: Skip to main content By Jake Kanter Jake Kanter International Investigations Editor @Jake_Kanter More Stories By Jake BBC Staff In Town Hall Revolt Over 1% Pay Rise, Job Cuts & Romesh Ranganathan Ad: “It’s Insulting” UK Sticks With Lisa Nandy As Culture Secretary, With Paramount-Warner & BBC Decisions Looming ‘Harry Potter’ Series Star Ruari Spooner Joins Bill Burr & Chris O’Dowd In Comedy ‘Bender’ View All July 21, 2026 2:39am BBC boss Matt Brittin BBC EXCLUSIVE : Is BBC director general Matt Brittin ‘s honeymoon period already coming to an end? Judging by the mood during a BBC town hall meeting on Monday, the realities of running the UK’s national broadcaster may be coming into sharper focus for the former Google man. Brittin and members of his top team were confronted with a barrage of unhappy questions from employees, who fired shots at plans for a below-inflation 1% pay rise, voiced anxiety about 2,000 layoffs, and lamented the low morale among colleagues. There was also some opprobrium for the big new BBC ad campaign, starring comedian Romesh Ranganathan . Related Stories News Gordon Ramsay Profiled In BBC Landmark Series 'Rise Of The Rockstar Chef' News UK Sticks With Lisa Nandy As Culture Secretary, With Paramount-Warner & BBC Decisions Looming In a written question that captured the mood, one employee said they are treated like “second-rate citizens” and that the 1% pay proposal is “an insult.” Another insider said the pay offer and job cuts are “unfair and demoralizing.” A third person pointedly asked: “A 1% pay rise will eat into my family’s finances so severely, I will have to cut back on something. What do you suggest I cut back on?” Watch on Deadline Others turned to black humor. “Great news that we’ve managed to pinch The 1% Club from ITV,” one employee wrote during the call. “Oh sorry, that’s just the pay proposal.” Bérangère Michel, the BBC’s chief financial officer, responded: “We know that 1% is not much. We wanted to do more, but we can’t afford it. So we implemented a minimum amount to protect those with the lowest pay, and that’s also why the executive team is not getting a pay increase this year.” The BBC is planning to slash costs by £500M ($670M) over the next three years, expanding on an existing target worth £1.5B. The bigger picture behind the cuts is that 94% of the UK population use the BBC every month, but fewer than 80% pay the £180 annual licence fee. The BBC is attempting to bridge this gap and can no longer dip into its cash reserves to fund operations. Brittin told staff that the BBC was encountering the same financial pressures as government departments, private sector companies, and charities. “We’re all having to be prudent, and that doesn’t make it easier for people, but we are all in a similar boat here,” he said. The pay rise is under discussion with unions, and speculation is already mounting that it could lead to industrial action. Some BBC employees compared the 1% pay rise with presenter earnings, which increased nearly £3M to a six-year high of £143M in the corporation’s last financial year. “Before he left, Scott Mills was on around £750k, far more than any other radio presenter. What contributed to this enormous salary? £750k could go so far and reduce the amount of staff leaving the BBC,” one insider wrote. A journalist proposed cutting by 10% the salaries of staff earning more than £100,000 to “avoid the current below-inflation 1% pay offer (in effect a pay cut) for the majority.” Brittin stressed it was “right” that the BBC offers competitive pay to attract talent, but the director general said he would examine the number of senior managers across the corporation. “We’ve said we’re definitely going to be looking at at least 10% savings on senior management roles,” he explained. “What I hear from some people, and I haven’t got deep enough yet, is that sometimes it feels like there are very many layers of oversight and not enough empowerment … that’s part of what we need to look at.” Others asked how much the BBC spent on its “What has the BBC ever done for me?” ad, starring Ranganathan. The campaign reboots a 40-year-old promotional video starring John Cleese, and is designed to showcase to UK viewers how much the BBC offers the nation. “How much did the new ‘What has the BBC ever done for me’ campaign cost? Totally excessive!” remarked an insider. Another added: “How much did the Romesh advert cost the BBC at a time we’re being told to both accept a 1% pay rise and be on constant alert that our job might be closed in the coming months?” Others questioned its content, bemoaning its failure to forefront local services, the World Service, and online offerings. On the cost of the campaign, the BBC’s chief customer officer Kerris Bright said: “It’s a really fair challenge, and we’re all facing those challenges. Marketing is facing the same challenges. We’re all having to look deeply at where we invest the resources that we do have and where we think we’ll get the best return for them.” She continued: “I think we need to invest in telling [audiences] the value that we create and how we do it. So I think yes, it is the right time to do that [advert] and to be careful with our investments. We’ve tried to be really smart in how we’ve done it.” Judge Grants TRO To Pause Proposed Merger For 14 Days Trek Continues With Year’s Best Monday After Stellar Opening Weekend ‘Scrubs’ Ups Ava Bunn To Series Regular Alongside Lead OG Trio; Updates On Season 2 Peter Lassally Dies At 93: Legendary Late-Night Producer Of Carson & Letterman Read More About: Comments

title: Anurag Kashyap, MIFFest Jury President, Searches for ‘a New Cinematic Language’
full_text: Jul 21, 2026 2:34am PT Anurag Kashyap, MIFFest Jury President, Searches for ‘a New Cinematic Language’ By Lin Ying-Hsuan Plus Icon Lin Ying-Hsuan Latest Anurag Kashyap, MIFFest Jury President, Searches for ‘a New Cinematic Language’ 2 hours ago APOS 2026 Takeaways: IP, Sport, AI Define Asia’s New Content Era 1 month ago ReelShort Looks Beyond Microdramas as Joey Jia Pushes for a New Entertainment Ecosystem 1 month ago See All Getty Images Indian auteur Anurag Kashyap , whose latest directorial feature “Bandar” marks his return behind the camera, is at this year’s Malaysia International Film Festival ( MIFFest ) as jury president while also leading a masterclass titled “Unfiltered Realism.” But rather than having all the answers, the veteran filmmaker is hoping to discover something new. “As jury president, what I’m really looking for is a new cinematic language,” Kashyap tells Variety . “I’m interested in seeing how younger filmmakers tell stories in ways my generation couldn’t.” Related Stories Fox's Red Seat Ventures, Amazon Strike Programmatic Advertising Partnership Ubisoft Hires Former Amazon Games Chief Christoph Hartmann as Head of New Division, Including Oversight of Acquired 'March of Giants' Game Kashyap believes today’s filmmakers are entering an entirely different creative landscape. AI tools, social media and an overwhelming abundance of information have transformed not only how films are made but also how audiences receive them. Popular on Variety Looking back on more than two decades of filmmaking, Kashyap pushed back on a common assumption. “Staying real isn’t a choice,” he added. “Whether I’m directing, producing or acting, you have to stay real. Only then can you fully play out reality and truly engage your audience.” He acknowledges that earlier in his career, limited budgets and available technology naturally pushed his films toward grounded storytelling. But he argues that authenticity has never been about visual style. Rather than seeing authenticity as a branding strategy, Kashyap believes local stories resonate globally precisely because they remain deeply rooted in their own cultures. Across Asia, Kashyap sees filmmakers share many of the same challenges despite working in different industries. “South Korea, Japan, China and India all have mature film industries,” he says. “But we’re all facing the same problem – audiences have shorter attention spans, and streaming platforms have created endless choices.” Those concerns echo comments Kashyap made earlier this year about the pressures facing contemporary cinema, particularly independent filmmaking in an increasingly fragmented viewing environment. Rather than offering a blueprint for filmmaking, Kashyap hopes the masterclass will become a conversation – one where he’s as eager to learn as he is to share his own experiences. “I have many questions myself as well,” he says. “I’m hoping to find some answers, have meaningful conversations during the festival and maybe discover another clue about the future of cinema.” ‘Scrubs’ Revival Ups Ava Bunn to Series Regular, X Mayo and Joel Kim Booster Exit Peter Lassally, Legendary Late Night Producer for Johnny Carson, David Letterman and Craig Ferguson, Dies at 93 ‘Stuart Fails to Save the Universe’ Limited Edition DC Comic Book, Experiential Space for Fans Planned for San Diego Comic-Con (EXCLUSIVE) Variety’s Faith & Media Impact Report 2026: Nominations Are Open Chloe Fineman Says ‘SNL’ Boss Lorne Michaels Was at World Cup Match When She Called to Say She Was Leaving the Show TIFF Announces Star-Studded Gala and Special Presentations, Led by ‘Rocky’ Biopic ‘I Play Rocky’ and Chris Rock’s ‘Misty Green’ Loading comments... title: Gordon Ramsay Profiled In BBC Landmark Series ‘Rise Of The Rockstar Chef’
full_text: Skip to main content By Max Goldbart Max Goldbart International TV Co-Editor @Goldbart1 More Stories By Max ‘The Portrait Of A Lady’: PBS Masterpiece, BBC & Fox Adapting Henry James Novel With Ensemble Cast Featuring Nell Tiger Free, Gemma Arterton, Bill Nighy, Jim Sturgess & Miranda Richardson Andy Burnham Becomes Seventh UK Prime Minister In 10 Years Ofcom Launches Investigation Into ‘Married At First Sight UK’ View All July 21, 2026 2:22am 'Rise Of The Rockstar Chef' BBC Gordon Ramsay is appearing in a landmark BBC series titled The Rise of the Rockstar Chef from his Studio Ramsay Global outfit. Ramsay is one of three chefs being profiled alongside Marco Pierre White and Heston Blumenthal. Set against the backdrop of the Cool Britannia era, The Rise of the Rockstar Chef will explore how the chefs’ unwavering ambition and intense rivalry reshaped Britain’s restaurant industry and redefined what it meant to be a fine dining chef. It will feature candid testimony from Ramsay and the other two as well as those who witnessed the fights, fall-outs and feuds first-hand, including renowned food critics, maîtres d’ and former commis chefs. Related Stories News UK Sticks With Lisa Nandy As Culture Secretary, With Paramount-Warner & BBC Decisions Looming Obituaries Edward Stourton Dies: UK Broadcaster Whose Most Recent Work Charted 250 Years Of American History Was 68 Ramsay is a huge star on both sides of the pond and has made hundreds of hours of cooking shows for Fox, which is selling the show via its distribution arm Fox Entertainment Global . Watch on Deadline Studio Ramsay Chief Creative Officer Lisa Edwards said: “Nineties and noughties nostalgia is at an all time high for both those of us who were there and those that weren’t – the heady mix of an uprising in fashion, politics and music makes it a decade like no other. But there was another rock and roll revolution happening in the UK. It was the decade that really put British food on the map led by the original Rock Star Chefs, Marco Gordon and Heston. Its the story of rivalry and thepursuit of perfection where the real heat was not just in the kitchen. The Rise of the Rockstar Chef was commissioned for BBC One and iPlayer by Catherine Catton, Head of Commissioning for Factual Entertainment and Events, while Ricky Cooper is BBC commissioning editor. The series director and producer is Zoë Dobson, the EPs are Edwards and Amanda Westwood, the executive in charge of production is Jess Holder and the line producer is Emma Pinching. title: All3Media International’s Jonathan Hughes Exits For ITV Studios After Banijay Merger
full_text: Skip to main content By Jesse Whittock Jesse Whittock International TV Co-Editor @WhittockTweets More Stories By Jesse All3Media International’s Jonathan Hughes Exits For ITV Studios After Banijay Merger ZDF & ProSieben Strike Streaming Pact As Traditional Rivals Team To Fight Streamers: “A Strong, Forward-Looking Signal” ‘The Earliest Show’ Revival Greenlit At Amplify Pictures & Stay Tuned With Kevin Carroll, Henry Winkler, Ryan Gaul & Brandon Scott Jones Joining Cast View All July 21, 2026 2:05am Jonathan Hughes Courtesy Another senior All3Media International exec is exiting the Midsomer Murder firm following its parent’s merger with Banijay . Jonathan Hughes is joining ITV Studios (ITVS) after ten years at All3Media International, and will take on the role of Vice President, Germany and Benelux Sales. ITVS has also promoted Louisa Forsyth to become Vice President, UK and Ireland Sales. All3Media’s parent, RedBird IMI, and Banijay earlier this month completed their merger , creating the world’s largest production studio outside of the U.S. majors. It also brought together two major program distribution businesses, with exits likely given Banijay’s management previously stating they would look to make savings from the unit. Related Stories News Lionsgate Shares Surge Amid Reports 'Hunger Games' Studio Is Courting Euro Buyers Executives Louise Pedersen, CEO Of 'The Traitors' Distributor All3Media International, Exits After Banijay Merger; Cathy Payne Remains Banijay Rights Chief The day after the merger completed, we were first with the news All3Media International’s long-serving CEO, Louise Pedersen, was exiting the merged division , which will be known as Banijay Rights. Cathy Payne, Banijay’s sales boss, is leading the unit as Chief Exec. Watch on Deadline There have been other senior exec exits, with All3 COO Sara Geater and Group Director of Corporate Development Steven Brown leaving. UK trade Broadcast reported Banijay CEO and Executive Director Patrick Holland is expected to take over the UK business, which will comprise 65 production houses. Hughes was All3Media International’s sales lead in German-speaking Europe, France and Benelux, shopping the likes of Midsomer Murders, All Creatures Great and Small , and Fleabag . He is credited with expanding All3Media’s German co-production footprint, securing pivotal deals for Van Der Valk, The Assassin, The Tourist, Boarders , and Generation Z , and oversaw format launches for The Traitors, Race Across the World , and Gogglebox . Prior to that, Hughes spent eight years as Sales Manager at factual specialist Optomen International, whose parent, Optomen, was acquired by All3 in 2010 for £40M ($53.7M). His new employer, ITVS, has also upped Forsyth to lead UK and Ireland sales from her previous role as Vice President of Global Content, Scripted. She has been with the company since 2018, having previously worked at Sky, which is now set to acquire ITVS’s parent ITV. Hughes and Forsyth will both report to Jemma Harvey, ITVS’s EVP, EMEA & UK Sales and Distribution. The appointments follow a spate of hires in ITVS’s unscripted team, namely Zack Wallace, Director, Unscripted, US & LATAM; Eliana Barbosa, Senior Sales Manager, Unscripted, Italy, Iberia, and CEE; and Ben Nohr, Sales Manager, Unscripted, France and Germany; with Lucy Pryke promoted from Sales Executive, Nordics, to Sales Manager, Unscripted, Nordics and Benelux. title: ZDF & ProSieben Strike Streaming Pact As Traditional Rivals Team To Fight Streamers: “A Strong, Forward-Looking Signal”
full_text: Skip to main content By Jesse Whittock Jesse Whittock International TV Co-Editor @WhittockTweets More Stories By Jesse All3Media International’s Jonathan Hughes Exits For ITV Studios After Banijay Merger ZDF & ProSieben Strike Streaming Pact As Traditional Rivals Team To Fight Streamers: “A Strong, Forward-Looking Signal” ‘The Earliest Show’ Revival Greenlit At Amplify Pictures & Stay Tuned With Kevin Carroll, Henry Winkler, Ryan Gaul & Brandon Scott Jones Joining Cast View All July 21, 2026 1:41am ProsiebenSat.1 Media and ZDF have struck a streaming pact Matthias Balk/picture alliance via Getty Images; Beata Zawrzel/NurPhoto via Getty Images ZDF and ProSiebenSat.1 are the latest traditional European rivals to team on a streaming deal. The pact will see ZDF’s on-demand content integrated into ProSieben’s streaming service, Joyn, from the fourth quarter of this year. This will allow Joyn users to access ZDF news, docs, culture, factual and entertainment programs for free without adverts. ZDF’s on streaming portal will remain “the central digital home of its public broadcasting content.” European players are increasingly looking to pool resources to combat the threat of global tech players, with recent examples seeing Channel 4 in talks with the BBC in the UK. ZDF and ProSieben noted their deal had been struck in an “increasingly international platform landscape” and that they were “deliberately focusing on a partnership that strengthens access to journalistic and cultural content ‘Made in Germany.'” Related Stories News Prime Video Drama 'The Assassin' Season 2 Lands Host Of Global Buyers News 'Deutschland 83' Co-Creator Jörg Winger Joins Dynamic Television To Head Up German Arm As ZDF Puts His New Show 'Conspiracy' Into Development Global streamers have also been striking deals with Euro broadcasters, with France’s TF1 recently rolling out its streaming and channel suite on Netflix, France Télévisions launching channels on Prime Video and Disney+ striking content sharing deals with ITV, RTVE, M6 and ZDF in the past year. Watch on Deadline The deal also ends a dispute between ZDF and Joyn from last year. That saw content from pubcasters ZDF and ARD added to Joyn without agreement, with ProSieben arguing free content was permissible to distribute under European law. Courts later ruled in favor of the public broadcasters, amid the introduction of Germany’s State Media Treaty, which encourages voluntary competition between pubcasters and commercial TV rivals. “Our mission is to reach as many people as possible with our high-quality content,” said Florian Kumb, Director Audience at ZDF. “By making ZDF streaming content available on Joyn in the future, we’re strengthening the visibility of public broadcasting journalism in the digital space.” Nicole Agudo Berbel, Chief Distribution & Partnerships Officer at ProSiebenSat.1: “This partnership demonstrates how public and private media can work together to find innovative responses to digital competition. In doing so, we’re sending a strong, forward-looking signal for the dual system and the media industry as a whole.” Judge Grants TRO To Pause Proposed Merger For 14 Days Trek Continues With Year’s Best Monday After Stellar Opening Weekend ‘Scrubs’ Ups Ava Bunn To Series Regular Alongside Lead OG Trio; Updates On Season 2 Peter Lassally Dies At 93: Legendary Late-Night Producer Of Carson & Letterman Read More About: Comments

title: Moebius Entertainment Picks Up World Rights To Fiona Roan Feng-I’s ‘Happily Ever After’ Ahead of Toronto Premiere
full_text: Skip to main content By Liz Shackleton Liz Shackleton pmc-media-editor-2024 More Stories By Liz Na Hong-Jin’s Cannes Competition Title ‘Hope’ Has Strong Opening At Korean Box Office PVR Inox’s Sanjeev Kumar Bijli On India’s Box Office Recovery, Cinema Expansion & Cannes Acquisitions Haul CJ ENM’s Mnet & Universal Music Group’s Republic Partner To Launch Global Girl Group View All July 21, 2026 1:27am Happily Ever After Hong Kong-based international sales company Moebius Entertainment has acquired worldwide sales rights (excluding Taiwan) to Happily Ever After , the sophomore feature from Taiwanese filmmaker Fiona Roan Feng-I . The film will make its world premiere in the Special Presentations section of the Toronto International Film Festival (TIFF). The acquisition marks a reunion between Moebius Entertainment founder and CEO Fred Tsui, director Fiona Roan Feng-i and producer Clifford Miu, following their collaboration on Roan’s debut feature, American Girl . Related Stories Festivals TIFF 2026 First Lineup: World Premieres Of 'I Play Rocky', Chris Rock's 'Misty Green', Anton Corbijn's 'A Talent For Murder', John Madden's 'Onwards And Sideways' & More Festivals TIFF Sets Special Presentations Including Riley Keough & Cate Blanchett Comedy 'Alpha Gang', Mahershala Ali's 'Your Mother Your Mother Your Mother' & More American Girl premiered at Tokyo International Film Festival in 2021 and received a global streaming release via Netflix following its international festival run. It also scooped five prizes at Taiwan’s Golden Horse Awards including Best New Director for Fiona Roan Feng-i, Best New Performer for Caitlin Fang, Best Cinematography for Giorgos Valsamis, the FIPRESCI Prize, and the Audience Choice Award. Watch on Deadline “ Happily Ever After confirms Fiona Roan’s remarkable talent as a filmmaker with a deeply human voice and an exceptional command of cinematic storytelling,” said Tsui. “After our rewarding collaboration on American Girl , I am delighted to be working again with Fiona and producer Clifford Miu. We believe the film will resonate strongly with audiences around the world, and we’re excited to introduce it to international buyers at Toronto.” Moebius Entertainment will launch international sales activities ahead of the film’s TIFF world premiere, with additional festival and distribution plans to be announced in coming months. Tsui, who was previously general manager and head of sales and international co-production at Media Asia, founded Moebius Entertainment as a boutique sales and consultancy company, working on auteur-driven Asian titles with strong international crossover potential. The company’s recent slate includes Peter Ho-sun Chan’s She Has No Name , Philip Yung’s Papa , Nick Cheung’s Peg O’ My Heart , Jame Hung’s My First Of May , and Wang Tong’s Wild Nights, Tamed Beasts . title: UK Sticks With Lisa Nandy As Culture Secretary, With Paramount-Warner & BBC Decisions Looming
full_text: Skip to main content By Jake Kanter Jake Kanter International Investigations Editor @Jake_Kanter More Stories By Jake BBC Staff In Town Hall Revolt Over 1% Pay Rise, Job Cuts & Romesh Ranganathan Ad: “It’s Insulting” UK Sticks With Lisa Nandy As Culture Secretary, With Paramount-Warner & BBC Decisions Looming ‘Harry Potter’ Series Star Ruari Spooner Joins Bill Burr & Chris O’Dowd In Comedy ‘Bender’ View All July 21, 2026 1:09am Lisa Nandy Getty Andy Burnham, the UK’s new prime minister, has decided to stick rather than twist with his choice of culture secretary. 10 Downing Street has announced that Lisa Nandy will remain in the role overseeing Britain’s film and TV industry, meaning she will extend her stay at the Department for Culture, Media and Sport beyond two years. She is the longest-serving culture secretary since Jeremy Hunt in 2010. Nandy was widely tipped to be reshuffled into a new role by Burnham. Even BBC director general Matt Brittin told staff on Monday that he thought there could be a new culture secretary. Related Stories News Judge Grants Temporary Restraining Order To Pause Paramount-Warner Bros Discovery Merger For 14 Days Obituaries Edward Stourton Dies: UK Broadcaster Whose Most Recent Work Charted 250 Years Of American History Was 68 She will continue to oversee a busy inbox, with her immediate priorities including whether to formally intervene in Paramount’s $110 billion takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery. Watch on Deadline Nandy will also be pivotal to the BBC’s future, as the corporation is currently in talks with the government over a new charter, which will effectively be its operating agreement for years to come. Other policy priorities include new legislation that would compel YouTube and TikTok to promote public service content from the BBC and other broadcasters. Some in the entertainment industry would have welcomed a change of culture secretary. Nandy is not viewed as being overly enthusiastic about her brief, with many senior industry figures describing her as aloof. She is, however, a supporter of the BBC, despite her deep misgivings about the Huw Edwards and MasterChef misconduct scandals, as well as editorial issues like the Glastonbury Gaza crisis. Nandy described the BBC as a “light on the hill” amid the tide of disinformation and said she wanted the corporation to have a “sustainable long-term funding model” under charter renewal. These positions are likely to continue, not least because the BBC will have an ally at the heart of government: James Purnell, its former policy and audio chief, will serve as Burnham’s chief of staff. Rahman Launches ARR Immersive App for Apple Vision Pro (EXCLUSIVE) 5 hours ago See All Studio TF1 America FAST channel provider wedotv has struck its first distribution deal in India, bringing four channels to Samsung TV Plus and reaching millions of Samsung Smart TV users across the country. The agreement adds wedotv Big Stories, wedotv Cars, wedotv Movies and wedotv Sports to the platform. wedotv Big Stories is produced in partnership with Big Media, while wedotv Cars is created with Motorvision. The lineup spans factual entertainment, automotive programming, film and sports content. Related Stories Ubisoft Hires Former Amazon Games Chief Christoph Hartmann as Head of New Division, Including Oversight of Acquired 'March of Giants' Game Fox's Red Seat Ventures, Amazon Strike Programmatic Advertising Partnership The deal extends wedotv’s push into Asia and follows the company’s broader international expansion since 2023. It also marks wedotv’s 13th territory for Samsung TV Plus channel launches, following recent rollouts in the Middle East and North Africa, Australia and New Zealand. Popular on Variety “The launch of our FAST channels on Samsung TV Plus in India marks an important step in wedotv’s international expansion and our continued investment in high-growth markets,” said Iza Piotrowska, senior VP of global business development, wedotv. “India represents a dynamic and rapidly evolving streaming market, and this partnership allows us to bring curated, globally relevant content to a highly engaged audience.” Samsung TV Plus counts more than 100 million monthly active users worldwide and comes pre-installed on Samsung Smart TVs and select devices without requiring a subscription. The service carries more than 4,300 ad-supported linear channels across 30 countries. “India is an important growth market for Samsung TV Plus, and we’re committed to building a compelling TV viewing experience by partnering with leading content providers,” said Kunal Mehta, general manager, head of business development, Samsung TV Plus India. “Adding wedotv’s portfolio broadens the range of premium international channels available on the service and reinforces our strategy of delivering high-quality, free streaming content to Samsung TV users across the country.” Wedotv is the advertising-supported streaming arm of Video Solutions AG, a Swiss media company, and operates channels available via its own website and apps as well as on partner platforms including LG Channels, VIDAA/Hisense, Zattoo and Freeview, among others. title: ‘The Odyssey’ Breaks Records With Massive Debut at U.K. and Ireland Box Office
full_text: Jul 21, 2026 12:10am PT ‘The Odyssey’ Breaks Records With Massive Debut at U.K. and Ireland Box Office By Naman Ramachandran Plus Icon Naman Ramachandran Latest FAST Provider Wedotv Launches in India With Samsung TV Plus (EXCLUSIVE) 3 hours ago ‘The Odyssey’ Breaks Records With Massive Debut at U.K. and Ireland Box Office 4 hours ago A.R. Rahman Launches ARR Immersive App for Apple Vision Pro (EXCLUSIVE) 5 hours ago See All ©Universal/Courtesy Everett Collection The U.K. and Ireland box office experienced a massive tentpole shift as Universal’s “ The Odyssey ” captured the top spot, storming the market with a spectacular debut weekend of £12.5 million ($16.8 million). The performance registered as Nolan’s second-biggest opening frame in the market, landing well ahead of “Oppenheimer.” Driven by an unprecedented showing on premium large formats, “The Odyssey” achieved the biggest opening Friday of the year and recorded Universal’s fifth-biggest opening Friday ever. The BFI Imax – the U.K.’s largest screen – reported its highest three-day opening weekend in history, bringing in a $294,249 from the single venue. This historic frame broke the previous all-time BFI Imax weekend record held by “Star Wars: The Last Jedi” ($289,682) in 2017, with “Oppenheimer” now sitting in third place with $277,936. To date, the London site alone has logged over 82,000 admissions, generating $2.9 million in ticket sales and maintaining a 94% occupancy rate, making it the venue’s third highest-grossing title of all time with 173 consecutive sold-out showtimes. Related Stories Fox's Red Seat Ventures, Amazon Strike Programmatic Advertising Partnership Ubisoft Hires Former Amazon Games Chief Christoph Hartmann as Head of New Division, Including Oversight of Acquired 'March of Giants' Game Disney claimed second place as “ Toy Story 5 ” brought in $2.2 million in its fifth weekend. The film’s five-week running cume is now $60.6 million. Disney also took the No. 3 spot with its live-action “ Moana .” In its sophomore session, the film added $2 million, taking its running total to $7.4 million. Popular on Variety Universal secured fourth place with “Minions & Monsters,” which took $1.5 million in its third week, pushing its cume to $12.5 million. Studiocanal’s horror feature “Evil Dead Burn” was in fifth place in its second weekend, banking $406,258 for a running total of $2.2 million. Black Bear’s ensemble comedy-drama “The Invite” occupied sixth place during its third weekend, adding $318,749 to its cume, which now rests at $3.3 million. Universal’s “Obsession” followed at No. 7 in its 10th frame, pocketing $249,169 to push its total to $24.9 million. Warner Bros.’ superhero holdover “Supergirl” hit eighth place in its fourth frame with $123,852, boosting its cumulative haul to $7.4 million. Universal’s Steven Spielberg sci-fi thriller “Disclosure Day” sat at ninth place with $91,904 in its sixth week, taking its total to $16.9 million. Paramount’s “Jackass: Best and Last” finished 10th in its fourth week, adding $57,313 for a total of $2.9 million. Looking ahead to the upcoming theatrical frame, Miracle/IHeart Cinema rolls out the animated family feature “Chickenhare And The Secret Of The Groundhog” across a wide saturation footprint of more than 300 screens. Park Circus is re-releasing the classic Aardman animation “Chicken Run” into over 100 locations, while Curzon has a 4K restoration rollout for Andrei Tarkovsky’s masterpiece “Nostalghia.” Trafalgar is opening Sia’s music event “Sia: Nostalgic For The Present (Concert)” and Altitude “Pinocchio Unstrung.” Verve Pictures launches Helen Walsh’s contemporary drama “On The Sea,” featuring a cast led by Barry Ward and Lorne Macfadyen. Picturehouse Entertainment is rolling out Giuseppe Tornatore’s fashion designer documentary “Brunello Cucinelli: The Gracious Visionary,” Mubi Ratchapoom Boonbunchachoke’s Cannes-winning supernatural black comedy “A Useful Ghost” and Dogwoof Ivy Meeropol’s documentary “Ask E. Jean.” Zee Studios International is opening “The India Story,” while Vue Lumière brings “Animal Farm” to select sites. title: A.R. Rahman Launches ARR Immersive App for Apple Vision Pro (EXCLUSIVE)
full_text: Jul 20, 2026 11:30pm PT A.R. Rahman Launches ARR Immersive App for Apple Vision Pro (EXCLUSIVE) By Naman Ramachandran Plus Icon Naman Ramachandran Latest FAST Provider Wedotv Launches in India With Samsung TV Plus (EXCLUSIVE) 3 hours ago ‘The Odyssey’ Breaks Records With Massive Debut at U.K. and Ireland Box Office 4 hours ago A.R. Rahman Launches ARR Immersive App for Apple Vision Pro (EXCLUSIVE) 5 hours ago See All ARR Immersive Entertainment A.R. Rahman , the two-time Oscar-winning Indian composer behind “Slumdog Millionaire” and “127 Hours,” is launching ARR Immersive , an app bringing his decade of immersive storytelling work to Apple Vision Pro on July 22. The app draws on Rahman’s ongoing work in immersive formats, tracing back to “Le Musk,” a 360-degree virtual reality film that debuted at the Cannes XR Festival. ARR Immersive marks the first time content from “Le Musk” has appeared on Apple Vision Pro, with the footage reworked for the headset’s spatial format. Related Stories Fox's Red Seat Ventures, Amazon Strike Programmatic Advertising Partnership Ubisoft Hires Former Amazon Games Chief Christoph Hartmann as Head of New Division, Including Oversight of Acquired 'March of Giants' Game The launch library also includes original content made exclusively for the platform. Three Kathak dance performances merge Western orchestral scores with Indian classical dance, featuring performers Pranith AP, Palak Chauhan and Mudra IV, with choreography by Shrita Baskar. ARR Live, a dedicated section devoted to Rahman’s concert performances, completes the initial lineup. Further titles are being developed for later rollout. Popular on Variety “For me, music has always been more than something we hear, it is something we feel emotionally,” Rahman said. “Technology now allows us to experience stories and music in ways that were unimaginable just a few years ago. With the ARR Immersive App, we wanted to create a space where audiences don’t simply watch a performance or listen to a composition, they feel present within it. This is about bringing together music, storytelling and innovation to create experiences that are deeply personal, emotionally engaging and truly immersive. I believe this is an exciting new canvas for creators, and this is only the beginning.” ARR Immersive can be downloaded through the Apple App Store, debuting first on Apple Vision Pro, with a Meta Quest release to follow. title: ‘Dancing With the Stars: The Next Pro’ Cast: All the Dancers Vying for a Spot on Season 35

Disney/Vince Valitutti Logo text [This story contains spoilers for episodes one and two of Dancing With the Stars: The Next Pro .] Dancing With the Stars is getting ready to welcome the addition of a new professional dancer at the end of Next Pro . The spinoff stars a group of 12 dancers who must compete in weekly challenges for a shot at becoming the next pro dancer on season 35 of Dancing With the Stars. Only time will tell who will win the series, securing a spot in the main series’ recurring cast. With new episodes airing Mondays, The Hollywood Reporter is breaking down all the details on the cast of dancers, who was eliminated each week, the judges and mentors. This story was originally published on July 14, and has been updated after each elimination. title: Chloe Fineman Says ‘SNL’ Boss Lorne Michaels Was at World Cup Match When She Called to Say She Was Leaving the Show
full_text: Jul 20, 2026 10:00pm PT Chloe Fineman Says ‘SNL’ Boss Lorne Michaels Was at World Cup Match When She Called to Say She Was Leaving the Show By Marc Malkin Plus Icon Marc Malkin Senior Editor, Culture and Events marcmalkin Latest Chloe Fineman Says ‘SNL’ Boss Lorne Michaels Was at World Cup Match When She Called to Say She Was Leaving the Show 6 hours ago Ed Harris Felt ‘Misled’ by ‘Dutton Ranch’ About ‘Inconsequential’ Role: ‘Get Me the F— Out of Here’ (EXCLUSIVE) 7 hours ago Daniel Radcliffe to Narrate Comedian Kyle Gordon’s Third Album (EXCLUSIVE) 4 days ago See All Variety via Getty Images “ Saturday Night Live ” boss Lorne Michaels was a bit tied up when Chloe Fineman called to say she was leaving the show. “In classic form, he was watching England vs. Argentina,” Fineman told Variety at Monday night’s premiere of Apple TV’s “The Dink” in Los Angeles. “He was like, ‘I’ll call you back. I’m a the game.’ And I was like, ‘The soccer game?’ Then I made a joke, I was like, ‘I assume you’re rooting for England because you’re energetically very British.’ Then he called me back, and we had a really lovely chat.” Related Stories Ubisoft Hires Former Amazon Games Chief Christoph Hartmann as Head of New Division, Including Oversight of Acquired 'March of Giants' Game Fox's Red Seat Ventures, Amazon Strike Programmatic Advertising Partnership Chloe Fineman says Lorne Michaels was at the World Cup when she called to tell him she was leaving #SNL : “He was like, ‘I’ll call you back. I’m at the soccer game.’ And I was like, ‘The soccer game? I assume you’re rooting for England because you’re energetically very British.’” pic.twitter.com/yXMV0zUP7r — Variety (@Variety) July 21, 2026 Fineman also shared that when the news of her “SNL” exit went live, she was very emotional. Popular on Variety “I cried when it was announced,” she said. “You really become a family [with the cast]… I wept when Colin Jost called me today, like, they really are my family. I’m happy that after seven years, I leave on a high note.” Fineman announced on July 16 that she was exiting the show. “After 7 wonderful seasons at ‘SNL’ I have decided it’s time for my next chapter,” Fineman wrote in an Instagram post . She concluded, “It’s really hard to leave ‘SNL’ but it does feel like the right time. I’m going to miss it a lot. But the people who work there are my family and that place is my home, and I know I’ll never be too far away.” Chloe Fineman says she cried after announcing she was leaving #SNL : “They really are like my best friends. So many people there. But it’s good to leave on a positive. You don’t want to stay past your expiration date.” pic.twitter.com/0W7uAJHVLW — Variety (@Variety) July 21, 2026 The actor-comedian joined “SNL” in 2019. She quickly became known for her range of impressions, which included Jennifer Coolidge, Timothée Chalamet, Drew Barrymore, Britney Spears and JoJo Siwa. Chloe Fineman says “we just scratched the surface” of her Melania Trump impression on #SNL . After announcing this week that she's leaving the sketch comedy series following seven seasons, Fineman says “you never know” when asked if she could return to play the First Lady during… pic.twitter.com/hniYeeG109 — Variety (@Variety) July 21, 2026 Fineman’s first post-“SNL” gig will be as a series regular role in the “ Myron Bolitar ” series currently in the works at Netflix . Series regulars will also include Jabari Banks (“Bel Air”) and Jamie McShane (“Task,” “Wednesday”). Ben McKenzie (“Everyone is Lying to You for Money,” “Gotham”) will appear in a recurring guest star role. Series leads include Colin Woodell, KJ Apa, and Diane Guerrero. Netflix originally announced the series pickup in May as part of their upfront presentation to advertisers. The show is inspired by Harlan Coben’s popular Myron Bolitar books, of which 12 have been published. The logline for the show states, “After an injury ends his NBA dreams, Myron Bolitar (Woodell) reinvents himself as a sports agent — using charm, smarts, and a ruthless partner to navigate the high-stakes and dirty world of sports, where saving his clients often means risking himself.” In addition to Fineman, the cast of “The Dink,” a sports comedy about pickleball, includes Jake Johnson, Ben Stiller, Christine Taylor, Patton Oswalt, Martin Kove, Mary Steenburgen and Ed Harris, as well as tennis legends Andy Roddick and John McEnroe. “The Dink” premieres on Apple TV on July 24. title: Jon Stewart Says the World Cup Final Was the ‘Reverse Super Bowl’: ‘The Players Spoke Spanish and the Halftime Show Was in English’
full_text: Jul 20, 2026 9:30pm PT Jon Stewart Says the World Cup Final Was the ‘Reverse Super Bowl’: ‘The Players Spoke Spanish and the Halftime Show Was in English’ By Jack Dunn Plus Icon Jack Dunn Latest Ed Harris Felt ‘Misled’ by ‘Dutton Ranch’ About ‘Inconsequential’ Role: ‘Get Me the F— Out of Here’ (EXCLUSIVE) 7 hours ago Ben Shapiro Calls ‘The Odyssey’ a ‘Masterpiece’ Despite Conservative Outrage: Christopher Nolan Is the ‘Best Working Director Today’ 2 days ago ‘Shark Tank’ Star Kevin O’Leary Sued For Claiming Utah Data Center Opposition Has Ties to China 2 days ago See All Comedy Central On Monday night’s episode of “ The Daily Show ,” Jon Stewart gave his post-game thoughts about Sunday’s World Cup final match, which saw Spain win out over Argentina 1-0. “Of course, the World Cup ended yesterday, finito, with Spain defeating Argentina 1-0 on the pitch, in the footy,” Stewart said at the top of the show. “The Spain-Argentina final was like a reverse Super Bowl, where the players spoke Spanish, and the halftime show was in English.” Related Stories Ubisoft Hires Former Amazon Games Chief Christoph Hartmann as Head of New Division, Including Oversight of Acquired 'March of Giants' Game Fox's Red Seat Ventures, Amazon Strike Programmatic Advertising Partnership The World Cup final halftime show, the first in the tournament’s history, featured Madonna, Justin Bieber and the Muppets. Stewart took note of “Sesame Street’s” The Count, who was there to celebrate the match along with Kermit and Miss Piggy. Popular on Variety “You got all the Muppets to choose from, why would in God’s name make the vampire work during the day?” Stewart joked. “Not only is it in the bright glare of the afternoon sun, but for most of the game, the score was 0-0! You’re taunting this poor bastard! ‘I sat in traffic for one, two, three hours for this.’” Stewart then, of course, shifted his attention to President Donald Trump’s presence at the game. He honed in on an awkward moment where Trump lingered on stage to be part of the trophy hoist, a moment usually reserved for “just the players.” “What the fuck are you doing on the stage?” Stewart exclaimed. “It’s for the players! Goddamn! Why must everything be tainted by the overinflated ego of Cankles Muldoon? Why?” Check out the entire monologue below. title: Sadie Sink Keeps Mystery Of ‘Spider-Man: Brand New Day’ Role & Reveals “I Am Not Aunt May”
full_text: Skip to main content By Armando Tinoco Armando Tinoco Staff Writer @armietinoco More Stories By Armando Mara Wilson Says ‘Matilda’ Sequel “Could Be Possible” & Why It “Annoyed” Her Being Linked To Film William H. Wisher Jr. Dies: ‘The Terminator’ & ‘Terminator 2: Judgment Day’ Screenwriter Was 71 Kevin Feige On Trying To Cast Adam Driver In Marvel Role Amid ‘X-Men’ Reboot Rumors: “It’s A Tradition To Zoom With Him & Then He Passes” View All July 20, 2026 8:59pm Sadie Sink and Spider-Man Sony Pictures Entertainment Sadie Sink ‘s role in Spider-Man : Brand New Day remains a mystery ahead of the film’s premiere. A new video teased Sink revealing three things about her character in the Sony Pictures production, but left audiences with the same doubts. “Since the trailer dropped, I’ve seen so many theories about who I’m playing,” Sink said in the Instagram clip. “To match your incredible energy, I’ve decided to give you three exclusive, confirmed answers right now.” Related Stories News Sadie Sink On Working With Tom Holland In ‘Spider-Man: Brand New Day’: “He Was Just So Relaxed And Open” News ‘Spider-Man: Brand New Day’ Red Carpet Photos: All Of Zendaya & Tom Holland’s Looks From Press Tour Starting off the video, Sink said, “I can confirm I am not Spider-Man.” She continued to joke and said, “I am not Aunt May,” the character that Marisa Tomei played and died in 2021’s Spider-Man: No Way Home . Watch on Deadline “And third, I can definitely confirm that I am in this movie,” she added. “As for who I really am, you’ll have to find out in the theater.” The Instagram caption for the post read, “We can confirm…she’s in this movie.” In a recent interview, Sink opened up about the positive experience she had working with Tom Holland in the superhero film. “It was interesting stepping into that space and being a little bit of an outsider in that way, but he could not have been more welcoming, and just the whole crew in general,” she told Nylon in an interview. “He was just so relaxed and open, and I felt very at ease.” The logline for Spider-Man: Brand New Day reads, “It’s a brand new day for Peter Parker. Fighting crime full-time as Spider-Man in a world that doesn’t remember him—and the pressure of seeing his old friends move on without him—sparks a change in Peter he may not have the power to control. But that transformation might also be the only thing that can stop a shocking new threat to the city and those he loves – a powerful villain no one can even see. The world may have forgotten Peter Parker, but he hasn’t forgotten them.” Sink will star in the film alongside Tom Holland, Zendaya, Jon Bernthal, Liza Colón-Zayas, and Mark Ruffalo. title: ‘Sherlock’ Creator Steven Moffat Set as International Guest of Serielizados – Barcelona Series Festival (EXCLUSIVE)
full_text: Jul 20, 2026 8:56pm PT ‘Sherlock’ Creator Steven Moffat Set as International Guest of Serielizados – Barcelona Series Festival (EXCLUSIVE) Moffat, also a ‘Doctor Who’ showrunner, will be joined by Sue Vertue, producer of his series and ‘Mr. Bean’ By John Hopewell Plus Icon John Hopewell Chief International Correspondent john_hopewell Latest ‘Sherlock’ Creator Steven Moffat Set as International Guest of Serielizados – Barcelona Series Festival (EXCLUSIVE) 7 hours ago World Cup Final Ratings Skyrocket Across Europe, Despite England and France Losses 19 hours ago Brazil’s Gramado Fest Set to Honor ‘I’m Still Here’ Star Selton Mello and ‘The Secret Agent’ Standout Maria Fernanda Cândido 2 days ago See All Steven Moffat, Sue Vertue Courtesy of Serielizados Currently priming “ Number 10 ,” one of the most awaited of British TV series, “Sherlock” creator and “Doctor Who” showrunner Steven Moffat will attend Serielizados -Barcelona Series Festival as its headline international guest. He will be joined producer Sue Vertue , producer of “Mr. Bean,” “Sherlock” and “Number 10” and CEO of Hartswood Films, the U.K.’s longest-established scripted producer, also behind ITVX and ITV1 hit “Douglas Is Cancelled.” Hartswood Films was majority acquired by ITV Studios in 2024. Related Stories Fox's Red Seat Ventures, Amazon Strike Programmatic Advertising Partnership Ubisoft Hires Former Amazon Games Chief Christoph Hartmann as Head of New Division, Including Oversight of Acquired 'March of Giants' Game Serielizados has yet to reveal what Moffat and Vertue will talk about. If a Series Mania masterclass is anything to go on, their session in Barcelona will most certainly be funny. Popular on Variety “Real life is funny – drama is a lie,” Moffat said at Serielizados in Lille. “You can’t be too funny, too sexy or too pretty. Always go for funny. Humor is truth on speed, that’s what it is. It’s insight with velocity.” British humor is one of the things the British can be very good at, and one of its television industry’s main export values. Serielizados is ever more aligned with the Catalan TV industry while still attracting the Spanish premieres of some of the most talked-about international series of the year – 2025’s festival was won, for example, by Norway’s “A Better Man” and Canada’s “Empathy” – the conference strand at Serielizados industry arm, Serielizados Pro, generated a high-quality debate energized top executives from Nordic broadcasters SVT, DR and YLE, plus also reps from BBC Studios, Arte France, Germany’s Beta Film and Paris-based Slot Machine. In-person attendance soared last year 80% to 16,000, while Serielizados’ online version, hosted by upscale SVOD service Filmin , hit 194,000 viewers. ‘In Vitro,’ ‘Episòdic’ In 2026, running Oct. 19-28, Serielizados will open with “In Vitro,” backed by Catalan public broadcaster 3Cat and HBO Max and set to debut this fall. Created by Marc Crehuet and Alejandra Guimerà, and directed by Creheut (“Pop Ràpid,” “The One-Eyed King,” !Mirror, Mirror”), it turns on Blanca, 37, a biologist, new to a fertility clinic where she confronts the needs and risks of her clients and her own as well. Beta Film picked up international distribution rights for“In Vitro” ahead of Series Mania. Serielizados closes with an experiement, the first season of “Episòdic: El principi d’Arquimedes,” a six-part anthology series, each episode lasting 15 minutes, made by 38 audiovisual students from universities, film schools and educational institutions. The series is directed by Ainhoa Lastra (“La nit del foc,” at the Universitat Pompeu Fabra, and Andrea Morales (Paraules d’amor) and Carlos R. Haro (“Cinc minuts més, si us plau”). The series is written by Daniel Durán Pous – a producer on “Unicorni” with two shorts to his credit, “Hipolit” and “L’ultim tren” – adapting the “The Arquimedes Principle,” from playwright Josep María Miró. An alleged child-abuse drama which cleverly plays with audiences own sense of prejudice, the work is hailed in its native Catalonia as a modern Catalan classic. Shooting ‘Episòdic’ ‘ Segunda Temporada,’ ‘Nissaga de poder,’ ‘Literaturium de séries ‘ Serielizados will also showcase “Segunda Temporada,” the latest Filmin Original from Spain’s upscale SVOD streaming service, turning on Paula, a once bestselling generational voice who, after writers’ block, is reduced to ghosting the bio of a washed up trap music legend, just out of prison, and maybe or maybe not feeling redemption. Created by Miguel Angel Blanca, behind breakout Filmin hit “Autodefensa,” “Segunda Temporada” will contend in Serielizados national competition. ‘Segunda Temporada’ The TV festival will also showcase the 30th anniversary of 3Cat daily soap “Nissaga de poder,” a milestone in its drive for ambitious originals, and “Literaturium de séries,” an exclusive live edition of the comedy podcast reviewing the supposed but fictional best TV series of the year. Mind The Gap Serielizados also announced Tuesday the second edition of Mind the Gap, its project pitching showcase which will take place Oct. 21, forming part of Serielizados PRO. Expanded to 11 selected titles, with its call for submissions now open and extended through Aug. 17, eight will be chosen from an open call and three tapped from winners at Germany’s Seriencamp Co-Production Pitch and Spain’s ECAM Forum and Iberseries & Platino Industria – an indication of how European events are working together to get projects in front of its top commissioners, production executives and sales agents who make up Mind the Gap’s jury. Participants can also expect press coverage. Winners at Mind the Gap’s inaugural edition last year were led by best series laureate “Fortune Cookie,” a low-fi sci-fi drama thriller set in Madrid’s Usera Chinatown, created by Adrián Saba (“The Erection of Toribio Bardelli”) and produced by Kubik Films, Newen Studios Spanish banner headed by brothers Fernando, Jorge and Alberto Sánchez-Cabezudo. “A comedy about two Gen Z misfits dodging eviction, chasing dreams, flipping the finger to adulthood and to the glittering city [Barcelona] that’s leaving them behind,” said producer Anna M. Bofarull, “Precarity Fair,” created by Eli Cruz and Mika Cilindro and produced by KaBoGa, won Mind the Gap’s Konga Music Award. In a further move, Serielizados has released its poster with a slogan, “An Eye for It,” which, it says, “emphasizes the role of its programming team in filtering and curating the ever-growing number of television series, helping audiences discover the most exciting and distinctive titles in an increasingly crowded landscape.” Serielizados has indeed often shown impecable taste in its lineup selection and top prizes, a jury recognising Sweden’s “Pressure Point” in 2024, for instance, as best international series. Plaudits in 2025 in the national competition went to “The Anatomy of a Moment” and “Jakarta.” World Cup Final Ratings Skyrocket Across Europe, Despite England and France Losses ‘Afterlife With Archie’ Series From ‘Riverdale’ Team Greenlit at Disney+ ‘Avengers: Doomsday’ Trailer: Doctor Doom Battles the MCU, X-Men and Avengers Face Off, Captain America Returns ‘A Different World’ Sequel Series Drops First Look at New Generation of Hillman Students Jalen Brunson Talks Taylor Swift’s Wedding, Giving Back and Whether the Knicks Will Visit the Trump White House Anurag Kashyap, MIFFest Jury President, Searches for ‘a New Cinematic Language’ Loading comments... title: Chloe Fineman Opens Up About Decision to Leave ‘SNL’: “I’m Really Proud of Everything, But It’s Hard”
full_text: × Skip to main content By Kirsten Chuba Plus Icon Kirsten Chuba Events Editor kirstenchuba Follow Sign Up View All July 20, 2026 8:48pm Chloe Fineman attends the premiere of Apple TV+'s "The Dink" at AMC Century City 15 on July 20 in Los Angeles. Frazer Harrison/Getty Images On Monday night, Chloe Fineman walked the carpet for new movie The Dink , following two big pieces of career news in the last week — first, that she was leaving Saturday Night Live , and second, that she would be joining Netflix show Myron Bolitar as a series regular. Fineman had appeared on SNL for seven seasons and wrote in her announcement post on Instagram that she “decided it’s time for my next chapter.” The new series — an adaptation of Harlan Coben’s thriller novels about an NBA star-turned-sports agent that is being co-showrun by David E. Kelley and Kyle Long — seems to be a big part of that next chapter, as she told The Hollywood Reporter at The Dink premiere. Related Stories TV 'Never Have I Ever' Alum Maitreyi Ramakrishnan Teases 'Elle' Season 2 Role: "Not Something I've Done Before" TV Chloe Fineman Finds Her First Post-'Saturday Night Live' Gig “Seven years is kind of like the classic way [at SNL ], and then like right as my contract was up, this amazing David E. Kelley opportunity came in like completely out of the blue. I did a little chemistry read and it was off to the races; it just felt like a perfect time,” Fineman explained. “I’m really proud of everything, but it’s hard. I’m genuinely really good friends with everybody and I genuinely couldn’t love that job more. So it’s really bittersweet, but I’m gonna be in New York. I’m down the street. Maybe you’ll see me.” She noted that Colin Jost had called her earlier that day because it was her birthday (“I fully cried”) and “everyone’s like really reaching out. It’s the sweetest show. It’s a hard show, but the people — it’s because of hours and everything. But like it really is a family, and I’ve been crying like sweet tears.” The comedian also added that she felt lucky to get to adjust to a more regular, non- SNL schedule while also working on something she was excited about, with filming starting on Myron Bolitar this week. As for larger SNL plans, Fineman said she was looking forward to “just stretching the TV wings and like who better than David E. Kelley? And I’m really excited to like watch SNL as an audience member [in person], which I’ve never done.” Her summer also includes the release of The Dink , starring alongside Jake Johnson and Ben Stiller in a comedy about a washed-up tennis pro who tries to save a club by reluctantly playing pickleball. Johnson joked that they “represent both sides” in the tennis vs. pickleball debate and the project came about when he was looking to do something “just funny and light and kind of easy listening.” “This kind of mid-range comedy with a nice three-act structure that’s kind of light has gone away, and they’ve either become really cool indies that are like very cool or huge franchises, and neither of those were the ones I really dreamt of making,” Johnson, who is a producer on the film, added of the sports comedy. “Being able to make a mid-tier comedy that’s just got like every scene there’s a different funny person who comes in — everybody’s funny in the movie. There’s heart. There’s a real story. There’s a structure. Like I want more movies like this, so I really hope it works.” The Dink , which also stars Mary Steenburgen, Ed Harris and Aaron Chen, premieres Friday on Apple TV. THR

title: Ed Harris Felt ‘Misled’ by ‘Dutton Ranch’ About ‘Inconsequential’ Role: ‘Get Me the F— Out of Here’ (EXCLUSIVE)
full_text: Jul 20, 2026 8:47pm PT Ed Harris Felt ‘Misled’ by ‘Dutton Ranch’ About ‘Inconsequential’ Role: ‘Get Me the F— Out of Here’ (EXCLUSIVE) By Marc Malkin , Jack Dunn Emerson Miller/Paramount+ Ed Harris does not sound satisfied with his role on “ Dutton Ranch .” At the Monday night premiere of Apple TV’s “The Dink,” Harris told Variety ‘s Marc Malkin that he felt “misled” about the importance of his role in the “Yellowstone” spinoff, and that halfway through filming Season 1, he was telling his team to “get me the fuck out of here.” “Prior to signing on for it, I was talked to quite a bit about what the season was, and what my character was going to do — and that I was one of the four main characters,” Harris said. “And that wasn’t really the case.” Related Stories Ubisoft Hires Former Amazon Games Chief Christoph Hartmann as Head of New Division, Including Oversight of Acquired 'March of Giants' Game Fox's Red Seat Ventures, Amazon Strike Programmatic Advertising Partnership Spokespeople for Paramount, series creator Taylor Sheridan and 101 Studios did not immediately respond to Variety ‘s request for comment. Popular on Variety Harris said he expressed his discontent, but did not name Sheridan. “I said, ‘I’m feeling underused and inconsequential,’ and they kind of went, ‘Oh,'” Harris added. “I’m being told my character will have a little bit more to do [in Season 2]. I was just a little frustrated, to tell you the truth.” Ed Harris says he felt “misled” about his “inconsequential” role on “Dutton Ranch,” and that halfway through filming Season 1, he was telling his team to “get me the fuck out of here.” “Prior to signing on for it, I was talked to quite a bit about what the season was, and what… pic.twitter.com/jwafO8ZUih — Variety (@Variety) July 21, 2026 Harris explained that when he actually watched “Dutton Ranch,” he thought it was “pretty good,” and he felt his character was “relatively important to the story.” However, during filming, “it didn’t feel that way.” On “Dutton Ranch,” Harris plays Everett McKinney, a good-humored veterinarian. Harris is currently signed on for two seasons. While Harris suggested he was ready to walk away from “Dutton Ranch,” it is unclear if he is actually looking to exit the show. The “Apollo 13” star then recalled filming a scene where his character sings in a bar, only to have the moment cut from the show. Harris said that “really pissed him off.” “So there’s this scene in there … I’m talking to [Beth Dutton] at the bar, and [Rip Wheeler] comes in and says, ‘OK, Everett, come on, it’s your turn,” Harris said. “I walk up to the mic, and they cut it. They cut the song out. I’m going, ‘Fuck you, people.'” Harris remembered the production telling him they cut the moment because it was “too ‘up’ for the dark ending of that episode.” But Harris said, at the time, he felt like that was “enough reason to say I’m voiding my contract.” “Dutton Ranch” launched its series premiere on Paramount+ on May 15. The show follows Beth Dutton (Kelly Reilly) and Rip Wheeler (Cole Hauser) as they leave Montana to build their own ranch in South Texas. Other cast members include Finn Little, Juan Pablo Raba, Jai Courtney, J.R. Villarreal, Marc Menchaca, Natalie Alyn Lind and Annette Bening. title: Mara Wilson Says ‘Matilda’ Sequel “Could Be Possible” & Why It “Annoyed” Her Being Linked To Film
full_text: Skip to main content By Armando Tinoco Armando Tinoco Staff Writer @armietinoco More Stories By Armando Mara Wilson Says ‘Matilda’ Sequel “Could Be Possible” & Why It “Annoyed” Her Being Linked To Film William H. Wisher Jr. Dies: ‘The Terminator’ & ‘Terminator 2: Judgment Day’ Screenwriter Was 71 Kevin Feige On Trying To Cast Adam Driver In Marvel Role Amid ‘X-Men’ Reboot Rumors: “It’s A Tradition To Zoom With Him & Then He Passes” View All July 20, 2026 8:05pm Mara Wilson TriStar Pictures Mara Wilson continues to be remembered for starring in the 1996 film Matilda , the Danny DeVito-directed film based on Roald Dahl’s 1988 novel of the same name. While attending the Fanboy Expo in Tennessee, Wilson opened up about a potential sequel to the fantasy film and why she didn’t like being associated with the character when she was younger. “People always ask me about doing a Matilda 2 . And that could be possible,” she said, according to People . Although Wilson is open to the idea of a follow-up to the film, she does question how they would make it happen. Watch on Deadline Related Stories News Mara Wilson Doesn't Want To "Change Myself To Fit Into Hollywood's Mold" After Child Acting Years News 'Matilda' Star Reveals Fears About 'Stranger Things' Kids Getting Caught In AI's "Deepfake Apocalypse" After Her Own Experience “But one thing that people always say is, ‘Oh, maybe it could be Matilda’s daughter.’ And I have some issues with that, because I think Matilda’s powers come from her adversity,” Wilson said at the expo. “They come from the bad things that she went through, and the whole point is that she overcame these things. And she found power in educating herself and in making friends with these people who were really lovely.” She continued, “And so I don’t think Matilda would raise her daughter to be in a position where she developed those powers to protect herself.” Wilson also raised up the question as to who would marry Matilda, adding, “Who’s good enough for Matilda? Matilda’s such an awesome character that I can’t imagine who she would marry and have kids with, you know?” Before starring in Matilda , Wilson also starred in 1993’s Mrs. Doubtfire and 1994’s Miracle on 34th Street. However, after starring in Matilda, people started to refer to her by her character’s name, which she wasn’t initially fond of. “I used to be a little annoyed when people would call me Matilda because I was like, ‘No, I’m Mara. It felt almost a little bit like being called by your sister’s name — your much cooler, smarter sister’s name. And I was like, oh, no, I’m not as cool as she is,” she recalled. Wilson remembered Carrie Fisher once said, “I feel like me and Princess Leia have kind of merged over the years,” and Wilson said that she “sort of feels like that too.” “I feel like Matilda has been a character that I looked up to, and Matilda would do things that I do. She would talk about mental health. She would talk about books. She would be working. She would be writing, she would be loving books and doing stuff for public libraries and things like that. And I do all of those things. Those things are very important to me,” she added. “So Matilda has really taught me a lot of good lessons. And I think I’ve become a lot like her in my own way.” Judge Grants TRO To Pause Proposed Merger For 14 Days Trek Continues With Year’s Best Monday After Stellar Opening Weekend ‘Scrubs’ Ups Ava Bunn To Series Regular Alongside Lead OG Trio; Updates On Season 2 Peter Lassally Dies At 93: Legendary Late-Night Producer Of Carson & Letterman Read More About: 1 Comments

title: William H. Wisher Jr. Dies: ‘The Terminator’ & ‘Terminator 2: Judgment Day’ Screenwriter Was 71
full_text: Skip to main content By Armando Tinoco Armando Tinoco Staff Writer @armietinoco More Stories By Armando Mara Wilson Says ‘Matilda’ Sequel “Could Be Possible” & Why It “Annoyed” Her Being Linked To Film William H. Wisher Jr. Dies: ‘The Terminator’ & ‘Terminator 2: Judgment Day’ Screenwriter Was 71 Kevin Feige On Trying To Cast Adam Driver In Marvel Role Amid ‘X-Men’ Reboot Rumors: “It’s A Tradition To Zoom With Him & Then He Passes” View All July 20, 2026 7:38pm William H. Wisher Jr. and 'The Terminator' Courtesy / Orion Pictures William H. Wisher Jr., a producer and screenwriter who co-wrote The Terminator and Terminator 2: Judgment Day , has died. He was 71. The Wisher family confirmed news of the filmmaker’s death, saying he “passed away peacefully at home.” Related Stories Obituaries 2026 Deaths Photo Gallery: Hollywood & Media Obituaries News ‘Terminator 2: Judgment Day’ Sets 35th Anniversary Global Re-Release “William believed that stories have the power to connect people across generations,” the family said in a statement. “While the world celebrates the unforgettable films he helped create, we will remember him for his kindness, his humor, his brilliant imagination, and the love he gave so freely to his family and friends. We are grateful for the outpouring of love and support during this difficult time.” Wisher helped redefine the science fiction action genre as one of the writers of James Cameron’s The Terminator , which was released in 1984. The screenwriter would return to work with Cameron in the film’s sequel, 1991’s Terminator 2: Judgment Day . Other films on which Wisher worked include Judge Dredd (1995), The 13th Warrior (1999), Exorcist: The Beginning (2004), Dominion: Prequel to the Exorcist (2005), Live Free or Die Hard (2007), and I.T. (2016). On hearing of Wisher’s passing, Terminator producer Gale Anne Hurd wrote, “Such terribly sad news…Bill was not only integral to THE TERMINATOR but he was such an all around great guy. RIP” Wisher is survived by his wife, Deborah Lynn Wisher; his siblings, Brian Wisher, John Wisher, Carla DeMaci, and Dana Aboissiere; and many nieces, nephews, cousins, extended family members, and friends.
