title: Adam Driver Has Passed on So Many Marvel Roles That It’s Become a “Tradition,” Kevin Feige Says
full_text: × Skip to main content By Carly Thomas Plus Icon Carly Thomas Senior Editor, Digital carly_thomas_ Follow Sign Up View All July 20, 2026 7:34pm Adam Driver Aurore Marechal/Getty Images Logo text It seems Kevin Feige has gotten used to Adam Driver telling him no. During a recent interview with Happy Sad Confused podcast host Josh Horowitz , the Marvel Studios president revealed that Driver has turned down joining the Marvel Cinematic Universe so many times that it has become a “tradition.” “It’s no secret we’d like to [work with Driver],” Feige said. “But it’s a tradition to Zoom with him and then he passes. That’s a long-lived tradition. He couldn’t be more cordial. He is usually upfront about how he is feeling.” Related Stories Movies 'Avengers: Doomsday' Trailer Reveals First Look at Robert Downey Jr.'s Doctor Doom Movies NY Film Festival Sets James Gray's 'Paper Tiger' as Opening Night Film The Marvel boss didn’t reveal which roles he’s pitched to Driver; however, rumors have been circulating on social media about the actor potentially boarding the big-screen reboot of the X-Men that’s currently in development. Marvel has yet to confirm any casting for the film, but The Hollywood Reporter reported last year that Jake Schreier was in talks to direct . Though Driver has never shared why he keeps turning down opportunities to join the MCU , it likely wouldn’t be because they’re blockbuster films, as he’s had his fair share in those. Most notably, he played Kylo Ren in the Star Wars sequel trilogy, including The Force Awakens (2015), The Last Jedi (2017) and The Rise of Skywalker (2019). In addition to other high-profile pics such as House of Gucci (2021), Ferrari (2023) and Megalopolis (2024), Driver likes to have a nice balance when it comes to his filmography, starring in plenty of indie films as well, including Inside Llewyn Davis (2013), Paterson (2016), Marriage Story (2019) and the upcoming Paper Tiger . Though Driver has no plans as of now to join the MCU, no matter how hard Feige tries, the actor is interested in stepping back into another blockbuster franchise: Star Wars . He had been working on a standalone Kylo Ren movie with director Steven Soderbergh , but Disney axed the film , despite it having Lucasfilm’s support. THR

title: ‘The Odyssey’ Rules The World, But Stephen Chow’s ‘Kung Fu Soccer’ Scores $214M+ Cume, ‘Toy Story 5’ Nears $1B – Global Weekend Top 10 Box Office
full_text: Skip to main content By Anthony D'Alessandro Anthony D'Alessandro Editorial Director/Box Office Editor @AwardsTony More Stories By Anthony ‘The Odyssey’ Rules The World, But Stephen Chow’s ‘Kung Fu Soccer’ Scores $214M+ Cume, ‘Toy Story 5’ Nears $1B – Global Weekend Top 10 Box Office ‘The Odyssey’ Doesn’t Stop: Epic Bound For Best Monday YTD Stateside With $17M+ – Box Office Update Sony Dates Bad Robot’s JT Mollner Horror Pic ‘Skeletons’ Starring Brie Larson For Summer 2027 View All July 20, 2026 6:49pm 'The Odyssey,' 'Kung Fu Soccer' and 'Toy Story 5' Universal Pictures / Disney Yes, Universal’s Christopher Nolan epic The Odyssey led by an extreme margin at the global box office with an updated $263.7M , a hands down opening record for the 2x Oscar winning filmmaker fueled by his best ever start overseas with $140.2M from 73 territories and his third best opening in North America with $123.5M. For more of the foreign breakdown on The Odyssey, click here . But here’s how everything else shook out with China elbowing its way into the chart with three, count ’em three blockbusters, despite Odysseus essentially No. 1 everywhere (except Vietnam and Argentina). He doesn’t show up in the Middle Kingdom until August 14. Why is China coming up so big at the B.O. suddenly? Because holidays are in full swing. Related Stories News 'The Odyssey' Doesn't Stop: Epic Bound For Best Monday YTD Stateside With $17M+ - Box Office Update News 'The Odyssey': What Do The Fates Have In Store For Christopher Nolan's Homeric Epic As Awards Season Gets Under Way? Kung Fu Soccer Star Overseas Stephen Chow’s Kung Fu Soccer per AG Reports was a definite No. 2 at the global B.O. with a second frame in China of $73.7M (501.1M Rmb) and a running cume of $214.3M (1,457M Rmb). Two other local titles triumphed over MPA ones there with the No. 2 pic in China being Pearl Studio’s Zhengxing Mu directed animation comedy fantasy All Wishes Come True with a 2-day weekend of $30.6M (207.8M Rmb) and running cume of $39.6M (269M Rmb) since opening on July 18. A solid result for this pic off its 9.7 Maoyan score. Pic’s blurb: Eight grassroot mortals use their street smarts and hilarious schemes to defy powerful gods, earning their title as the “Eight Immortals.” All Wishes come true was No. 6 at the global weekend B.O. Also big in China was the Yingxin Ma directed and written female drama, A Part of Me , from Enlight Pictures which began with $13.6M (92.4M Rmb) ranking third in the country, and No. 8 on the global weekend chart. What was the highest grossing Hollywood movie in China this weekend? Universal’s Minions and Monsters with a third weekend of $3.6M and running cume of $40.3M; still the pic’s biggest offshore territory both weekend and total wise. Watch on Deadline Third belongs to Disney/Pixar’s Toy Story 5 which at $957.3M is ever so close to becoming the third movie this year to pass $1 billion worldwide after Universal/Illumination’s Super Mario Galaxy and Lionsgate/Universal’s Michael ($1.01B). Broken out, Toy Story 5 has a running domestic total of $429.87M , which is now (for the moment), the highest grossing movie in North America YTD ahead of Super Mari o Galaxy Movie ($429.81M). Toy Story 5 posted a global weekend of $43.7M ($13.76M domestic, $30M overseas from 52 markets). Foreign running cume stands at $527.5M. The pic was the No. 1 (non-local) film for the weekend in Japan (running total $41.2M) and Argentina (running cume $18.3M). Mexico is the top grossing territory still outside North America with $71.4M while UK/Ireland is second with $60.2M. Disney’s live-action Moana posted a second global weekend of $42.9M ($17.9M domestic, $25M from 50 territories) in fourth place around the world for a running cume of $177.4M WW ($81M domestic, $96.4M int’l). title: FIFA World Cup Halftime Show Director on Making First-Ever Concert: “Challenging Beyond Challenging”
full_text: × Skip to main content By Ethan Millman Plus Icon Ethan Millman Music Editor View All July 20, 2026 6:28pm Ronaldinho, Madonna and Ronaldo perform during the Topps Final Halftime Show in the FIFA World Cup 2026 Final match between Spain and Argentina at New York New Jersey Stadium on July 19, 2026, in East Rutherford, New Jersey. Carl Recine/Getty Images Hamish Hamilton has directed over a dozen Super Bowls , the opening ceremony to the 2012 Summer Olympics, six Emmy Awards ceremonies, four Oscars and three Grammy nights, among many more marquee events. None of those, he says, were quite as challenging as the first-ever FIFA World Cup Finals Halftime Show he directed on Sunday. “A lot of that is because it was the first,” Hamilton, co-founder of the production company Done + Dusted, tells The Hollywood Reporter over Zoom from his New Jersey hotel room the morning after the World Cup, calling the show “challenging beyond challenging.” Related Stories TV Was the World Cup Final Halftime Show "Too American"? Rest of the World Seems to Think So: "Glib and Unsubtle" TV Five Things the Cameras Didn't See at the World Cup Final “Along with the Olympics, this was right up there,” he says. “I’m sure the first Super Bowl Halftime show was incredibly difficult, but when I came in they’d already done over 40 of them. By the time you get to Super Bowl 47, the NFL’s got everything super well drilled. But in this kind of scenario, there are so many unanswered questions. There’s all the people who haven’t done it before, a huge amount of nervousness, we’re doing this in daylight, you’ve got the grass, you’ve got a huge collection of superstars. It’s absolutely terrifying because there are so many things can go wrong.” Luckily for Hamilton and FIFA, they didn’t have any technical snags Sunday, and months of prep came and went in a quick-fleeting moment as Madonna, BTS , Justin Bieber, a Gustavo Dudamel-led orchestra, Shakira, Burna Boy, the PS22 Choir and the Muppets all hit the stage in an 11-minute time span. “It’s a mixture of exhaustion, relief and happiness,” Hamilton says of the show’s conclusion. “I’m very proud of what we did to help bring Chris [Martin, of Coldplay, who served as curator] and Global Citizens’ vision to life. They had this dream four years ago. It’s an enormous undertaking and they should be applauded for what they did.” Figuring out how to format the show to fit so many acts in such a short time frame was its own challenge, but as Hamilton says, the bigger question was the “gargantuan kind of logistical undertaking” involved with the broader production. There was the “very strong daylight that just kept coming,” which influenced how they shot the whole show. And whereas the NFL games MetLife Stadium usually hosts are played on artificial turf, the FIFA final was played on grass. The more delicate surface impacts everything for halftime, from what stage sets are possible to what sorts of cameras they could use. Plenty of the pressure was coming from outside as well. The halftime show was the subject of skepticism from soccer fans around the globe, who questioned the need for the show and worried about how adding a concert to a game where halftime is normally just 15 minutes would impact the competition itself. The Cure’s Robert Smith made headlines last week ahead of the finals in his critique of the show, writing “pleasejustfuckoff.” The Guardian , meanwhile, referred to the concert as “ the half-time show nobody asked for.” Hamilton, himself British and a lifelong fan of the Premier League’s Arsenal F.C., acknowledged those sentiments and noted they’re more prevalent in markets outside of the U.S., which is historically much more acclimated to melding music and sport thanks to events like the Super Bowl. “I’m a football fan, and have been my entire life,” he says. “Around the world, certainly around Europe and in the U.K., I saw some people who were like, ‘We don’t want your halftime show. We don’t want the Americanization of the beautiful game.’ I was, of course, very aware of that resistance. It’s not so much in America, because sports, entertainment and music sit a lot closer together here than they do probably anywhere else in the world. But I was very aware of that, and also about disrupting the second half if there were any issues with the grass. It’s hard, it’s pressure.” Will any skeptics be converted now that the first show has come and gone with the rest of the tournament? “It’s a very good question, I was so absorbed and immersed in creating the show within the game,” Hamilton says. “If someone else was directing and I was just watching from London [as a fan], maybe I could comment. But listen, it’s the World Cup Final, it’s the greatest, most-watched sporting event in the world. Having been part of it as a live director, I would say on balance definitely, and I hope that they do another one.” THR

title: Kevin Feige On Trying To Cast Adam Driver In Marvel Role Amid ‘X-Men’ Reboot Rumors: “It’s A Tradition To Zoom With Him & Then He Passes”
full_text: Skip to main content By Armando Tinoco Armando Tinoco Staff Writer @armietinoco More Stories By Armando Mara Wilson Says ‘Matilda’ Sequel “Could Be Possible” & Why It “Annoyed” Her Being Linked To Film William H. Wisher Jr. Dies: ‘The Terminator’ & ‘Terminator 2: Judgment Day’ Screenwriter Was 71 Kevin Feige On Trying To Cast Adam Driver In Marvel Role Amid ‘X-Men’ Reboot Rumors: “It’s A Tradition To Zoom With Him & Then He Passes” View All July 20, 2026 6:03pm Kevin Feige and Adam Driver Getty Images Kevin Feige has been trying to work with Adam Driver and cast him in a Marvel film role, but so far it has not become official. Amid rumors that Driver could potentially star in the X-Men reboot, the studio head revealed where he’s at in finally working with the Girls actor. “It’s no secret we’d like to [work with Driver],” Feige said in the Happy Sad Confused podcast. “But it’s a tradition to Zoom with him, and then he passes. That’s a long-lived tradition. Related Stories News Marvel’s Kevin Feige Says He Feels “Like A Gigantic Loser & Failure” Over Failed ‘Blade’ Reboot With Mahershala Ali Trailers ‘Avengers: Doomsday’ Trailer: Russo Brothers Enlist The Entire Marvel Universe In Their Comic Book Return Feige said that Driver “couldn’t be more cordial” in his conversations about working with Marvel Studios, adding, “He’s usually upfront about how he is feeling.” Watch on Deadline Filmmaker Jake Schreier is set to direct the X-Men reboot from a script being developed by Beef creator Lee Sung Jin and The Bear showrunner Joanna Calo. Rumors that Driver is set to join the cast of the reboot have been circulating for weeks, with conflicting reports that the Star Wars: The Last Jedi actor is up for roles like Magneto, William Stryker, or Mister Sinister. Marvel is set to introduce a new generation of X-Men actors in the reboot after Avengers: Doomsday says goodbye to the actors who played the mutants across the Fox era. All the characters now live under the Disney umbrella following Disney’s 2019 acquisition of 21st Century Fox. In a 2025 interview, Schreier discussed what he learned on Thunderbolts* that he would apply to making the X-Men film. “The biggest learning curve for me was the proportion of the action to the more emotional, character-driven scenes, and how, even though it’s more shooting days than I’ve ever had, they get eaten up quite quickly by the action stuff,” he told Empire . “By the time we got to the end of it, it felt like, ‘Oh, now I feel like we get how to do this a little bit better.’” Judge Grants TRO To Pause Proposed Merger For 14 Days Trek Continues With Year’s Best Monday After Stellar Opening Weekend ‘Scrubs’ Ups Ava Bunn To Series Regular Alongside Lead OG Trio; Updates On Season 2 Peter Lassally Dies At 93: Legendary Late-Night Producer Of Carson & Letterman Read More About: 2 Comments

title: Matt Damon Reveals Why He Had Female Stunt Double for ‘Odyssey’ Scene: “Greatest Arms I’ve Ever Seen”
full_text: × Skip to main content By Carly Thomas Plus Icon Carly Thomas Senior Editor, Digital carly_thomas_ Follow Sign Up View All July 20, 2026 5:55pm Matt Damon as Odysseus in 'The Odyssey.' Melinda Sue Gordon/Universal Pictures As Josh Horowitz points out, Matt Damon had quite an impressive physique in The Odyssey , specifically in the bicep region. However, the actor confessed he can’t take full credit for all the times you see his muscular limbs in the film. During a recent interview with the Happy Sad Confused podcast host , Damon revealed that he actually had a female stunt double for one key scene in Christopher Nolan’s epic action fantasy film . After thanking Horowitz for the “high praise,” The Rip actor explained, “When we did the forced perspective stuff of the Laestrygonians — I don’t know if there’s been a thing about how we did it — there were these stunt men who were all seven feet,” he said, referring to the giant warriors in The Odyssey . Related Stories Business What's Really In the AI Odyssey Film Movies Christopher Nolan on 'The Odyssey' Whetting His Horror Appetite, Making the "Ultimate Dog Movie" and Re-Releasing 'The Prestige' “And then they got stuntmen who were under five feet, and so my double was a woman, a female stunt performer, who had the greatest arms I’ve ever seen,” he added. Damon recalled first meeting the stunt double in the catering tent while filming and going up to hug her and thank her “for all the work she put in.” “So there are those shots in the movie where you see these giants looming over me, and so it was almost 100 percent my arms in the movie, but you gotta give it up where it’s due,” he said. The Odyssey , based on Homer’s ancient Greek poem, follows King Odysseus’ (Damon) dangerous voyage back to Ithaca after the Trojan War, including battles with mythical creatures, as he attempts to reunite with his wife, Penelope (Anne Hathaway), and son, Telemachus (Tom Holland). The film also stars Zendaya, Robert Pattinson, Charlize Theron, Lupita Nyong’o, John Leguizamo , Elliot Page, Mia Goth, Corey Hawkins, Jon Bernthal , Samantha Morton, Travis Scott and Benny Safdie. Following its July 17 release, the film scored a major box office victory , collecting more than $264 million globally in its debut weekend. THR

title: Peter Lassally, Producer for Johnny Carson and David Letterman, Dies at 93
full_text: Skip to main content By Mike Barnes Plus Icon Mike Barnes Senior Editor mikebarnes4 Follow Sign Up View All July 20, 2026 5:08pm Peter Lassally Mark Mainz/Getty Images for AFI Peter Lassally, a concentration camp survivor who became known as the “Host Whisperer” for producing talk shows headlined by Johnny Carson , David Letterman and Craig Ferguson , has died. He was 93. Lassally died Sunday of natural causes at a memory care facility in Los Angeles, his son, Tom Lassally, the partner, producer and manager at 3 Arts Entertainment, told The Hollywood Reporter . Lassally had produced programs starring Arthur Godfrey and Mike Wallace before joining Carson on NBC’s The Tonight Show , then based in New York, in 1969. He served as the quiet behind-the-scenes guy in a producing partnership with the boisterous Fred De Cordova and remained with the show until Carson’s final broadcast on May 22, 1992. Related Stories TV Sylvia Bugg, PBS Programming Chief, Dies at 55 Music Jim Gilstrap, Singer on 'Good Times' Theme and Stevie Wonder's "You Are the Sunshine of My Life," Dies at 79 After just one week off, Lassally jumped to Late Night With David Letterman as an executive producer for what would prove to be the NBC program’s final season. When Jay Leno was picked over Letterman to replace Carson, Lassally advised Letterman what to do next. “I said to Dave, ‘If you’re going to follow Jay Leno at 12:30 when he gets The Tonight Show , you’re going to hate yourself, you’re going to be miserable,'” Lassally recalled in a 2009 chat for the Television Academy Foundation website The Interviews . “You’re going to think, ‘Why am I following Jay Leno when I for 12 years hosted the 12:30 show … and my reward should be The Tonight Show ?’ If you agree, I’ll try to get you an 11:30 show — not knowing when I said that just how I was going to do that.” Lassally hooked Letterman up with powerful CAA agent Michael Ovitz, and the host fielded lucrative offers in syndication, cable and broadcast before deciding to move to CBS in 1993 to topline Late Show With David Letterman from the Ed Sullivan Theater on Broadway at 11:35 p.m. The deal included ownership of a 12:35 a.m. program, The Late Late Show , and Lassally returned to California to set up that program with host Tom Snyder. When Snyder left after four years in March 1999, Lassally disapproved of his replacement, the snarky Craig Kilborn — he had pushed for Jon Stewart — and stepped aside. But when Kilborn quit in a salary dispute in 2004, he got his man in the charming Scottish actor-comedian Ferguson. “I always had a good relationship with the hosts that I worked with because I would tell them the truth. That has really worked well for me. That’s how I got the title the ‘Host Whisperer,'” the two-time Emmy winner said. “I’m not good at pumping sunshine up their rears. If I didn’t like it, I can’t fake it, I can’t pretend it was good. I’m very lucky the hosts respected that.” Peter Eduard Lassally was born on Oct. 14, 1932, in Hamburg, Germany. His father, Paul, was an importer/exporter in the coffee business, and his mother, Elsa, was a housewife. He and his family fled Germany in 1938 on the way to England to escape the Nazis but “didn’t go far enough,” settling in Amsterdam, where he went to the same grade school as Anne Frank. After his father died and the Germans invaded the Netherlands, his mother and older sister were confined to two concentration camps from 1943-45. The second camp, Theresienstadt in Czechoslovakia, “was a very strange place,” Lassally told NPR in 2012. “That’s where the Nazis fixed up the camp with all sorts of phony, wonderful decor and invited the Red Cross to come and visit and see what wonderful things they were doing.” In 1947, Lassally came to live with an uncle in Canton, New York, where he attended St. Lawrence University after graduating from high school in Queens. Back in New York City, he worked as an usher at Radio City Music Hall and as a page (alongside Regis Philbin ) at NBC. Lassally reviewed Broadway plays for a WOR radio program hosted by Ruby Mercer and went on to produce her show, then produced the NBC radio program Nightline , where he hired Mort Sahl , Shelley Berman and Nichols & May to do short comedy bits. “That’s where I found doing what I really love more than anything else, finding talent and developing them,” he said. Lassally produced Godfrey-hosted shows on CBS radio and television — he eventually became president of Godfrey’s production company — and in 1961 moved to PM East , a syndicated talk show from Westinghouse that was hosted by Wallace. He regularly booked Barbra Streisand on that because she lived in an apartment above the studio; she hadn’t even begun her singing career yet. Lassally got to know Carson’s second wife, Joanne, when she asked him to produce a TV pilot for her, and she helped him get a job on The Tonight Show . De Cordova came aboard a bit later, and they enjoyed a complementary relationship, he told Mark Malkoff on a 2014 episode of The Carson Podcast . “He would be Mr. Jolly and schmooze with the guests, which I didn’t like to do, and I would do all the work. And that worked perfectly,” he said. The pair had lunch together every day for more than two decades, often throwing in a game of poker. Lassally had final say on all Tonight Show guests — he recognized Garry Shandling ‘s potential during an appearance on The Merv Griffin Show and pursued Steven Wright after seeing him do stand-up at a Chinese restaurant — and broke in the guest hosts who sat in when Carson was on vacation. “The gift that I keep looking for in people, whether they’re hosts or guests, is likability,” he said. “I don’t care if you have the No. 1 record or the No. 1 movie; if you’re not likable, I’d rather not put you on because I don’t think the audience will stay up.” Joan Rivers eventually became the permanent guest host, and she assumed Lassally would follow her when she landed her own late-night show at Fox in 1986. He stayed loyal to Carson, however. After Carson retired, Lassally convinced him to send jokes to Letterman for his monologues. “He didn’t want to force Dave to use them because of who Johnny was,” he told Malkoff. “I said, ‘No, these jokes are wonderful, and Dave would be honored and proud to do them on his show.’ He finally gave in.” Shandling also bounced talk-show ideas off him for HBO’s The Larry Sanders Show . “Shandling and his writing partner would ask me, ‘Could this have happened?’ I would say yes and no,” he said. In his TV Academy Foundation interview, Lassally noted that Late Show originally was to spend just one year in New York before moving to Los Angeles. However, when the program came out of the gate beating Leno in the ratings, those plans were shelved, and Lassally stayed in New York for about 2 1/2 years, longer than he would have preferred. He was serving as a consultant on Late Late Show when he was asked to oversee the selection of a new host after Kilborn’s exit. Four finalists — Ferguson, D.L. Hughley, Michael Ian Black and Damien Fahey — emerged from more than two dozen guest hosts (among them Stewart) before Ferguson, despite his heavy Scottish burr, got the job. Lassally retired in 2014 as executive producer of Late Late Show when Ferguson left. He collected 13 Emmy nominations during his career, with wins in 1992 for The Tonight Show and 1994 for Late Show . “When Johnny Carson and David Letterman needed counsel and an honest opinion, they immediately went to Peter Lassally, the professor of late night,” NBC said in a statement. “His relationship to each of these giants was immeasurable, and both of them would undoubtedly say his contribution to their success was so impactful that it would be difficult to put in words. [He was] a quiet man whose influence and stature spoke volumes; his brilliance was in shaping what we watch, how we watch and why late night was, and remains, vitally significant.” In addition to his son, survivors include his daughter, Andrea, and his grandchildren, Walker, Eloise, Annabel and Owen. His wife of 60 years, Alice, predeceased him. “I respect talent, I’m in awe of talent,” he once said. “If you are really talented, I will put up with your craziness. Because if you’re that talented, you’re not a normal person; there’s something wacky about you.” THR

title: ‘Never Have I Ever’ Alum Maitreyi Ramakrishnan Teases ‘Elle’ Season 2 Role: “Not Something I’ve Done Before”
full_text: Skip to main content By Hilary Lewis Plus Icon Hilary Lewis Deputy Editor, East Coast hilarylewis Follow Sign Up View All July 20, 2026 4:14pm Maitreyi Ramakrishnan at the 'Elle' world premiere in New York. Dimitrios Kambouris/Getty Images Logo text An open casting call. A virtually unknown actress selected to lead a coming-of-age streaming TV sho w executive produced by an ambitious veteran actress-turned-producer with a strong personal connection to the IP. That’s what Maitreyi Ramikrishnan went through when the Canadian actress was chosen among many to lead the Mindy Kaling -executive produced, semi-autobiographical Netflix comedy Never Have I Ever . But the same could also be said about Lexi Minetree , the Atlanta native who was selected from roughly 5,000 audition tapes , again via an open search, to play the star of the Reese Witherspoon executive-produced Legally Blonde prequel, Elle . Related Stories TV Whether It Be Romantic Lead or Writer-Director, Jack Martin's Ready to Tackle Whatever Role Comes Next Lifestyle Hollywood's Top Docs Reveal Their Cosmetic Secrets at THR's A-List Aesthetics Event: "We Have to Be Scientists, Artists, Technicians and Psychologists" Now, three years after the end of Never Have I Ever , Ramakrishnan is going back to the world of teen shows to play the nemesis to Minetree’s Elle Woods in the already renewed (and already filmed) season two of Elle . Though Ramakrishnan’s role might seem like she’s starring in yet another high school series after Never Have I Ever ‘s four-season run, she teases that her addition to the Legally Blonde universe is different. “Without spoiling too much, I like my character’s dynamic in the gang because it’s not something I’ve done before,” Ramakrishnan told The Hollywood Reporter at the recent Elle premiere in New York. Ramakrishnan’s character, Sam, has been described as “possessing all our hero’s ambition and none of her optimism. She’s the founder, editor-in-chief and lord baron of the school newspaper, the commissioner of the grammar police and the enemy of joy,” the description continues. “If Cosmo is Elle’s bible, the AP Stylebook is Sam’s holy grail.” Speaking about what drew her to the role, Ramakrishnan mentioned the “iconic” world of Legally Blonde and called Witherspoon a “legend.” Having worked with Witherspoon on the already filmed second season of Elle and Kaling on Never Have I Ever , Ramakrishnan also shared her thoughts on how the actresses-turned-powerhouse-producers, aiming to improve representation onscreen, are similar. “The biggest thing they have in common, I would say, is that they are both women with audacity, and that’s why I think they hire women with audacity as well,” she said. “Meeting Lexi is such a joy because she works so hard. She’s just amazing. Game recognizes game, and I think Mindy knew what she did casting me, and Reese knew what she did casting Lexi Minetree.” Ramakrishnan described both Kaling and Witherspoon as “hard-working women who want to tell good stories.” All eight episodes of Elle ‘s first season are streaming on Amazon’s Prime Video. THR

title: Hunter Biden’s Image Rehab Tour Takes a Hard Right
full_text: × Skip to main content By Kevin Dolak Plus Icon Kevin Dolak View All July 20, 2026 4:06pm Hunter Biden, son of President Joe Biden. Anna Moneymaker/Getty Images Hunter Biden ‘s ongoing rehabilitation tour is about to take a hard right turn with an unorthodox sit-down with Nick Fuentes . His conversation with the far-right influencer is slated to premiere on web channel Channel 5 News later this month. The much-discussed interview — which nearly ended in fisticuffs — appears to be part of the former first son’s pivot toward life as an edgy but earnest media personality. Or is it more PR spin from Biden, who is rewriting his chaotic personal story to cast himself as a victim of the Republican smear machine? Biden has been working with an L.A.-based homelessness prevention nonprofit and is starting a foundation for addiction aftercare, since he was disbarred in Connecticut and Washington, D.C. last year following his 2024 conviction on federal gun-possession and tax-evasion charges. He has also spent the past year building a second act as a visual artist. Related Stories News Hunter Biden, Nick Fuentes Interview Nearly Turns Physical, Report Says Business Michael Strahan, Podcaster? 'GMA' Star to Co-Host Show Under His SMAC Banner He’s now slowly stepping back into public view, but clearing his name doesn’t appear to be his only motive. Last week, he won $1.7 million in a defamation suit against former Overstock CEO and 2020 election denier Patrick Byrne, who’d falsely claimed Biden solicited an $800 million bribe from Iran in 2021. He’s fighting the lies, but he’s also owning the mistakes. Not that his motives aren’t being questioned. “There’s an enormous freedom in owning your own story,” Biden told Gavin Newsom last month on the governor’s podcast, after the two joked about each other’s presidential ambitions. He also defended his appearance on right-wing provocateur Candace Owens’ show , telling Newsom he hoped Owens could see the humanity in his situation. “I don’t think these are bad people,” he explained. Now a painter rather than an attorney and globe-trotting lobbyist, Biden splits time between South Africa and the U.S. You are most likely to see him, however, working a growing podcast circuit — including Friends Keep Secrets , Armchair Expert and This Is Gavin Newsom and Candace , among them. But it was his August 2025 interview with Channel 5’s Andrew Callaghan — where he opened up about his addiction, the laptop scandal and the 2020 election — that set his next move in motion: the two are reportedly teaming up on a podcast, giving Biden a full-time pulpit to talk recovery, politics or whatever he likes. A month ago, Biden and Callaghan approached Callaghan’s publicist, Mitchell Jackson — the former Vice wunderkind turned go-to fixer for mostly right-wing disruptors — about landing a heavy-hitting conversation that could “move the needle” in American politics, a source familiar with the plans told The Hollywood Reporter. It was that same day that Jackson found himself on the phone with Fuentes. The far-right pundit — who in 2022 finagled a Mar-a-Lago dinner with President Donald Trump , and now commands his growing following of vocal bigots — was looking for an image rehab interview, Jackson said, hoping to explain how his views have evolved past the antisemitic material that built his brand. Who better to arrange it than the publicist behind Owens, the reigning queen of online controversy? Jackson pitched both camps on staging the interview inside a seedy motel — a wink at the 2019 photos of Biden in the New York Post, where he appeared shirtless and smoking a crack pipe in a motel room. But no Philly-area motel would let them film, so they built a set to match. Biden, Jackson said, had signed off on the concept fully aware of what it referenced. His real motive for sitting with Fuentes, per Jackson, was reach: “The way he explained it was that he wanted to speak with Nick because Nick is one of the most influential people with young men in America. Which is true.” Fuentes commands an unusually devoted audience. His self-styled “Groypers,” named for a Pepe the Frog variant, is a symbol embraced by the far-right since roughly 2016. Fuentes has spent years stoking racism, nativism, sexism, homophobia, Islamophobia and antisemitism, deploying that army with ease, most often against Charlie Kirk’s Turning Point USA, which Fuentes considered insufficiently “America First.” Jackson believes Fuentes, 28, is now trying to put distance between himself and his past remarks. He’s evolved, Jackson argues — he says he never supported violence, never identified as a Nazi, and the “edgelord” trolling of his teenage years isn’t who he is now, even if his America First politics haven’t moved an inch. “I went and looked at some of the stuff he said recently on the show. I needed to watch it outside of clips going viral,” Jackson said. “It was very clear he’s been criticizing his own audience — if anyone would bother to watch, instead of just the clips sent to them by people who hate him.” The interview — already deemed notorious by a press corps that hasn’t seen it — premieres this week. Whether it’s the swing that launches Biden into the podcast big leagues remains to be seen. Until this recent run of appearances centered on sobriety and his personal scandal, Biden kept a low profile, communicating mostly through a recently launched Substack and avoiding cameras in the wake of the controversial pardon his father issued on the way out of the Oval Office (after promising he wouldn’t). Hunter is back now, and being far more candid than anyone might expect from a scandal-plagued former president’s son. In June, he joined X with an opening line built for virality: “I’m Hunter Biden. You’ve never actually heard from me.” (Never mind that his 2021 addiction memoir, Beautiful Things , became a bestseller once the GOP weaponized it). Since his return to public life, he’s leaned into confrontation — sparring with Trump immigration adviser Stephen Miller (he later clarified he’d meant to call Miller “sadistic, infantile” and something cruder, not merely “ugly”) and with CNN’s Jake Tapper over a jab at stepmother Jill Biden’s memoir — while staying self-aware enough to needle himself, too (“I am not posing nude,” he told a Playboy writer angling for a Q&A). Jackson, for one, sees strategy here: Biden is reaching across the aisle in a way Democrats broadly haven’t. “I think the Republicans have been much better at finding common ground with people; the Democrats have been much more resistant to doing that,” he said. “If the Democrats have a brain, they’re going to watch the episode on Friday and send Andrew Callaghan a thank-you note. But I don’t know if Democrats have a brain.” Yet there’s an entirely different read on Biden’s media blitz. In a scathing New York Post column last week , Miranda Devine — author of the data deep dive book, Laptop From Hell — argues that Biden’s press tour is really a promotional run for two things at once: a forthcoming documentary and a new, more convenient answer to the question of his infamous laptop. The documentary, which had cameras, follows Biden from 2021 through his 2024 pardon, was produced by Kevin Morris, the Hollywood lawyer and longtime Biden benefactor who loaned him millions of dollars over the same years the cameras were rolling and who has separately bought pieces of Biden’s artwork (as he explained when dragged into a closed-door meeting with House Republicans). The film, Devine writes, posits that the “laptop from hell,” with its evidence of the Biden family’s Ukraine dealings, among other alleged scandals and all that motel footage, was never a laptop at all, but a database stolen and doctored by Rudy Giuliani, or possibly Russian operatives, or maybe both, then passed off as real with the help of Trump allies and a psychologist who once treated Biden. The forthcoming documentary is directed by S.R. Bindler, of the 1997 cult favorite and Morris-produced Hands on a Hardbody, and coming soon, per Bindler’s website. But last week, Biden sent the stolen-laptop theory wide after previewing the claim in a recent Wired interview promoting his forthcoming book, with posts on X and his Substack. “They called it the laptop. I will call it that too, although the name is not accurate, the provenance was never established, and no court or forensic examiner ever certified the chain of custody that would have made it evidence rather than theater,” he wrote on X, throwing to his Substack, where he writes that “a scandal was constructed, and a laptop was produced to anchor it.” But what about the crackpipe? The motel room trysts? The stolen database does contain real footage of his, he says — the evidence of the years-long struggle with addiction that has become central to his new public persona. “Twenty years of my life, or a version of it. Messages sent at hours that don’t belong to anyone sober. Photographs I would never have taken in daylight,” he shared. “It is accumulation — the slow daily work of an addict doing the thing that is killing him because the alternative feels like dying faster.” THR

title: Ryan Reynolds Confirms Another ‘Deadpool’ Movie
full_text: × Skip to main content By Carly Thomas Plus Icon Carly Thomas Senior Editor, Digital carly_thomas_ Follow Sign Up View All July 20, 2026 4:04pm 'Deadpool and Wolverine' Everett Collection Logo text It looks like Ryan Reynolds will be putting on the Deadpool suit once again. The actor confirmed during a recent appearance at Fanatics Fest 2026 that another Deadpool movie is coming, which would follow the box office success of 2024’s Deadpool & Wolverine . “There’s a few really deep cuts that I think are missing behind the movies. I think there’s a few deep cuts missing from some of the comics. There’s certainly some stuff that, you know, I love that Fabian Nicieza [co-creator of Deadpool] wrote that is incredible. Gerry Duggan, who was a friend, was an incredible comic writer,” Reynolds said onstage, via a video shared on X. “There’s stuff upcoming. There’s eventually, you know, remotely another Deadpool film. It’s going to be great.” Related Stories Movies 'Mayday' Directors Break Down Trailer for Ryan Reynolds, Kenneth Branagh Cold War Buddy Movie Lifestyle As Taylor Swift Gets Married, Former Best Friend Blake Lively Is Out of Sight The actor-producer didn’t give any additional details on whether the film would be another solo Deadpool installment or more of an ensemble film, with his chaotic antihero becoming more of a supporting player. The Hollywood Reporter has reached out to Marvel for comment. Last year, THR exclusively reported that Reynolds was exploring ideas for another movie in the franchise; however, it would feature X-Men characters. The actor has also previously stated that he did not see another solo Deadpool movie in his future. “Centering Deadpool works best if you take everything away from him and put his back against the wall. I can’t really do that again,” he told THR in 2024. “A fourth time feels a little iterative and redundant. That doesn’t mean sacrificing fun. There is still an arc for Deadpool which is fulfilling and powerful.” When Deadpool & Wolverine , starring Reynolds and Hugh Jackman as Wolverine, was released in 2024, it went on to become the top-grossing R-rated movie of all time , collecting over $1.3 billion globally. The film followed 2016’s Deadpool and 2019’s Deadpool 2 , which were also massive box office successes. THR
